Inter-universal Teichmuller theory

IUT as a map of alien arithmetic worlds.

Inter-universal Teichmuller theory is Mochizuki's proposed arithmetic deformation theory for number fields equipped with elliptic-curve data. It replaces direct comparison of ring/scheme structures by a network of Hodge theaters, theta links, log links, indeterminacies, and log-volume estimates.

Enter the wiki Open source library Collas category view Lean efforts
Global route source objects to Diophantine bounds

Orientation

IUT is not best approached as a single calculation. It is a theory of controlled comparison between arithmetic structures that are deliberately not kept inside one global ring or scheme history. The vocabulary is unusual because the comparison method is unusual.

Objects

The starting objects are initial theta data, hyperbolic orbicurves, Frobenioid-type structures, log-shells, and Hodge theaters.

Motion

The central motion is through theta links and log links, arranged in the log-theta lattice. These links compare weakened data rather than ordinary scheme identities.

Output

The proof-critical output is a log-volume comparison in IUT III, Corollary 3.12. IUT IV then applies this comparison to Diophantine inequalities.

Wiki convention. This page explains the source theory and its formalization interfaces. It does not assert that the disputed proof is settled. When a statement is a conditional interface, the page says so.
Related: reading map source library glossary

Reading Map

The best first pass is thematic, not chronological. Read the overview texts to understand why alien copies and separated structures are introduced; then read the four IUT papers with the route map below in mind.

PassThemePrimary local sourcesGoal
1 Global picture Panoramic Overview, Mutually Alien Copies, Collas overview Know why IUT uses alien copies, non-scheme-theoretic deformation, and anabelian reconstruction.
2 Source machinery IUT I, IUT II Track initial theta data, Hodge theaters, theta values, Kummer theory, and theta links.
3 Proof-critical corridor IUT III, Essential Logical Structure Locate Theorem 3.11, Step (x), Step (xi), and the Corollary 3.12 comparison.
4 Consequences IUT IV, Explicit Estimates Understand how log-volume estimates are converted into Szpiro, abc, and Vojta-type bounds.
5 Verification and critique Scholze-Stix, 2018 discussion report, 2026 Lean report Separate source claims, objections, responses, and formalization targets.
Four-paper layer map what each paper contributes to the route

Official Mochizuki IUT Corpus

The local library includes the ten papers listed under the Inter-universal Teichmuller Theory heading on Mochizuki's official papers page. The wiki treats these as the primary source spine; auxiliary papers are used for orientation, critique, formalization, and independent checks.

No.PaperWiki roleLocal source
1IUT I: Construction of Hodge TheatersInitial theta data, Frobenioids, theta-Hodge theaters, D-prime-strips.PDF
2IUT II: Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic EvaluationTheta evaluation, mono-theta environments, Gaussian monoids, theta links.PDF
3IUT III: Canonical Splittings of the Log-Theta-LatticeLog-theta lattice, Theorem 3.11, indeterminacies, Corollary 3.12.PDF
4IUT IV: Log-Volume Computations and Set-Theoretic FoundationsExplicit log-volume estimates, Diophantine consequences, species/mutations.PDF
5Panoramic Overview of IUTConceptual orientation for alien copies and non-scheme-theoretic deformation.PDF
6Bogomolov's Szpiro Proof from the IUT ViewpointComparison with the geometric Szpiro proof and the role of theta/Gaussian data.PDF
7Mathematics of Mutually Alien CopiesExtended explanation of alien copies, Gaussian analogies, and comparison discipline.PDF
8Explicit Estimates in IUTNumerical effective estimates attached to the IUT IV output layer.PDF
9Essential Logical Structure of IUTLogical decomposition of the proof and the AND/OR structure around the dispute.PDF
10IUT as an Anabelian GatewayShort report linking IUT to anabelian geometry and analytic number theory.PDF
Coverage checkpoint: all ten official IUT-category Mochizuki PDFs are present locally and linked from this wiki. The next expansion pass can therefore focus on concept pages instead of source acquisition for this core corpus.
Related: full source library formalization route paper atlases

Invitation Route

The expanded Invitation notes give a compact route into IUT before the four main papers: Hodge-Arakelov motivation, Teichmuller-style deformation, log-theta-lattice structure, and the need for anabelian reconstruction across non-ring-compatible links.

Four-section route from Hodge-Arakelov motivation to inter-universality

Hodge-Arakelov motivation

The notes start from a hypothetical global multiplicative subspace and generators for an elliptic curve. The Hodge-Arakelov calculation suggests the assignment {q^{j^2}} -> q and motivates the desired height estimate.

Invitation §1height

Teichmuller-style deformation

Mochizuki compares IUT to Teichmuller deformation: one keeps a common underlying structure while dilating one dimension. In the arithmetic setting, value groups are dilated by theta-function data while unit data is left undilated.

Invitation §2dilation

Log-theta lattice sketch

Section 3 presents the log-theta lattice as a noncommutative two-dimensional diagram of Hodge theaters. Vertical log links and horizontal theta links correspond to the two combinatorial dimensions of local arithmetic structure.

Invitation §3lattice

Inter-universality and anabelian geometry

The log and theta links are not compatible with ring structures. On the opposite side of such a link, local fundamental groups and Galois groups make sense as abstract topological groups, so anabelian reconstruction is required to compare the alien ring structures.

Invitation §4anabelian
Sources: Invitation to IUT, expanded Related: classical roots route Hodge theaters theta/log links log-theta lattice anabelian background

Panoramic Route

The 2012 panoramic overview presents IUT as a response to a precise obstacle: scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory suggests the needed height estimate, but its useful "global multiplicative subspace" picture is not generally available. IUT replaces that unavailable scheme-theoretic situation by non-scheme-theoretic deformations and then measures the resulting distortion.

Four-part route from Hodge-Arakelov motivation to Diophantine output

Hodge-Arakelov obstruction

Section 1 reviews an idealized Hodge-Arakelov theorem whose codomain contains factors weighted by q^{j^2}. If global multiplicative subspaces and canonical generators existed generally, a height bound would follow by conventional arithmetic line-bundle arguments.

Pano §1motivation

Key assignment

The overview isolates the assignment from the family {q^{j^2}} to q as the central computational problem. Since it is not a ring homomorphism inside one conventional scheme theory, IUT treats the two sides as living in distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures.

Pano §1KEY

Coricity

Coricity is the invariance of selected structures under the deformation. The overview compares it to the underlying real-analytic surface in classical Teichmuller theory: it is the part that remains usable while holomorphic structure is changed.

Pano §1core

Prime-strips as sections

Section 3 explains prime-strips as valuation-indexed sections of Spec(K) → Spec(F). This simulates the missing global multiplicative subspace and global generator data, while forcing explicit bookkeeping of arithmetic line-bundle and Frobenioid structures.

Pano §3prime-strips

Theta-link non-ring map

In the overview, the theta-link at bad places is an isomorphism of abstract monoids sending the theta-value family to the q-parameter side. Its compatibility with local Galois groups is by an unspecified abstract isomorphism, not by an identity map of one ambient ring.

Pano §3non-ring

Jacobi/Gaussian analogy

The overview relates multiradiality of the etale theta function to the invariance of the Gaussian under Fourier transform and to Jacobi's identity. The analogy marks the role of quadratic theta data and logarithmic unit geometry in the final log-volume calculation.

Pano §4theta analogy
This panoramic route is a navigation layer. The detailed source objects are still the Hodge-theater, prime-strip, theta-link, log-link, multiradial, and log-volume constructions in the four IUT papers.
Sources: Panoramic Overview Related: mutually alien copies Hodge theaters prime-strips theta/log links log-theta lattice

Bogomolov-Szpiro Analogy

Mochizuki's 2016 paper compares IUT with Bogomolov's proof of the geometric Szpiro conjecture. The analogy is not a claim that the proofs are the same: it isolates a common pattern in which a precise degree computation must be combined with nontrivial estimates made in a larger geometric container.

Shared comparison pattern precise identity plus displacement/log-volume estimates

Upper-half-plane geometry

The paper formulates Bogomolov's proof through the geometry of the upper half-plane, universal coverings of SL2(R), and displacement estimates for lifted unipotent elements.

Bogomolov §1-3geometry

Degree plus estimate

The geometric proof combines a precise degree relation with nontrivial displacement estimates to bound orders of q-parameters. Mochizuki compares this with IUT's combination of a precise theta-link degree relation and multiradial log-volume estimates.

Bogomolov §3-4Szpiro

IUT similarities

The analogy identifies upper-half-plane boundary geometry with the additive symmetry part of Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters, and compares Bogomolov's displacement containers with IUT's mono-analytic log-shell containers.

Bogomolov §4containers

Essential difference

Section 5 stresses the absence, in Bogomolov's proof, of Gaussian distributions and theta functions. These are precisely central in IUT: theta values, Galois evaluation, and processions are not optional decorations.

Bogomolov §5theta functions

Frobenius/etale analogy

Mochizuki compares holomorphic line-bundle and discriminant-modular-form data in Bogomolov's proof with Frobenius-like objects, and local systems plus fundamental groups with etale-like objects.

Bogomolov §4Frobenius/etale

Formalization use

For the wiki and Lean work, the paper is best used as an analogy for proof shape: precise correspondences alone do not replace estimates, and estimates alone do not provide the exact source comparison.

audit useanalogy only
Sources: Bogomolov-Szpiro viewpoint Related: panoramic route mutually alien copies log-theta lattice IUT IV consequences

Classical Roots Route

Mochizuki's Classical Roots lecture notes give four classical analogies for reading IUT: isogeny invariance of elliptic-curve heights, crystals and Hodge filtrations, complex Teichmuller dilations, and the Jacobi theta identity. The page uses them as orientation maps into the source papers, not as replacements for the formal IUT constructions.

Four classical roots source analogies pointing into IUT machinery

Isogeny-height GMS model

In the hypothetical presence of a global multiplicative subspace, quotienting an elliptic curve by it gives a relation like q_E^l = q_E*, hence l log(q_E) = log(q_E*). The lecture presents Hodge theaters as the apparatus for generalizing this key point when such global objects do not exist.

Classical Roots §1GMS

Hodge-filtration container

The crystals/Hodge-filtration section uses p1*E ~= p2*E as a common container large enough to house two Hodge subbundles but small enough to yield a nontrivial height estimate. Mochizuki compares this to IUT's multiradial common container for incompatible ring structures across the theta link.

Classical Roots §2container

Complex Teichmuller dilation

Classical Teichmuller deformation keeps a common real-analytic structure while one real direction is dilated and the other is fixed. The lecture uses this to motivate the log-theta lattice's infinite-H portion, where log links act like rotations of arithmetic holomorphic structure and theta links perform the dilation-like move.

Classical Roots §3dilation

Jacobi theta identity

The identity theta(t)=t^-1/2 theta(t^-1) is read as descent to a quotient up to a factor. The lecture compares the identity with IUT's multiradial representation, the factor with indeterminacy, and the involution with the log-link rotation of holomorphic structure.

Classical Roots §4theta
Use in the wiki. These analogies are particularly useful for readers before the technical Frobenioid and Hodge-theater pages. They explain why IUT repeatedly asks for a common container that is broad enough for alien structures but narrow enough to preserve a numerical estimate.
Sources: Classical Roots of IUT Mutually Alien Copies Panoramic Overview Related: GMS/GCG motivation Hodge theaters log-theta lattice multiradiality theta/log links

IUT III Packets, Kummer, log-volume local holomorphic tensor packet, local mono-analytic tensor packet, global tensor packet, tensor-packet integral structures, weighted log-volume measure, tensor packets source page, holomorphic n-packet formula, mono-analytic packet compatibility, local LGP monoids source page, single packet monoids, LGP splitting monoids act on packets, vertically coric LGP source page, single Kummer map not enough, bad-prime root-of-unity indeterminacy, global packet Frobenioids source page, FMOD/Fmod two models, theta/q pilot objects, LGP/lgp log-theta lattice source page, log-shells alone are not enough, packet log-volume source page, forced procession average, realified semi-simplification, global Kummer source page, roots-of-unity non-interference, FMOD/Fmod bias, Theorem 3.11 source page, Theorem 3.11 data (a),(b),(c), Ind1/Ind2/Ind3 in Theorem 3.11, source IPL/SHE/APT, Corollary 3.12 proof steps, single theta-link rule, Step (x) coarse space, Step (xi-f) membership, local packet Frobenioids, local LGP monoids, vertically coric LGP monoids, upper-semi local clauses, bad-prime splitting compatibility, log-Kummer totality, FMOD/Fmod concrete models, global packet Frobenioids, theta/q pilot objects, LGP Gaussian log-theta lattice, why log-shells alone are not enough, packet log-volume definition, mono-analytic log-volume compatibility, global log-volume compatibility, forced procession average, log-link/log-volume compatibility, q-power log-volume, global Kummer non-interference, FMOD/Fmod bias, F⊥ prime-strip compatibility, F×μ prime-strip square, Theorem 3.11 multiradial representation, Theorem 3.11 log-Kummer correspondence, Θ×μ-LGP link compatibility, IPL/SHE/APT/HIS, label-to-procession transitions, log-volume coarse space, log-Kummer concept page, log-Kummer correspondence, upper semi-compatibility, packet log-volume, holomorphic hull IUT III, Section 3

Essential Logical Structure Boolean-logical route AND/XOR chain, redundant-copy warning, Stp1-Stp8, log-Kummer centrality, common-container chain, label reconstruction Essential Logical Structure, Section 3

Mutually Alien Copies Expository model two-copy method, Gaussian analogy, GMS/GCG motivation, q-power model, mono-anabelian transport, closed-loop prime-strip, AND/OR alien model Mutually Alien Copies, Sections 1-3

Hyperbolic orbicurve

The quotient of a once-punctured elliptic curve by its order-two involution is a basic orbicurve input. It is the geometric object behind many fundamental-group and cusp reconstructions.

Tempered fundamental group

Tempered fundamental groups encode covering data used in IUT I's anabelian reconstruction layer. They are distinct from the later mono-analytic comparison interfaces.

Decomposition subgroup

Decomposition subgroups recover local valuation and cusp information from group-theoretic data. They are part of the source machinery for local prime-strip objects.

Model base-NF bridge

The model base-NF bridge is the number-field side of the bridge formalism in IUT I. It transports capsule data while preserving the appropriate multiplicative labels.

Model base-Theta bridge

The model base-Theta bridge is the theta side of the same bridge discipline. It is tied to evaluation sections and label-class transport at bad places.

D-ThetaNF-Hodge theater

A D-ThetaNF-Hodge theater combines compatible D-NF and D-Theta bridges over a shared capsule of D-prime-strips.

Holomorphic procession

A procession organizes bridge and prime-strip data over label-indexed capsules. Later procession-normalized log-volumes inherit this label discipline.

ThetaNF-Hodge theater

A ThetaNF-Hodge theater glues theta and number-field bridge structures into a combined Hodge-theater interface. It prepares the later additive constructions.

Theta cyclotome

Definition 5.2 uses theta-side cyclotomes to compare Kummer classes of coric rational functions with roots of unity attached to the Frobenioid side.

Theta bridge

Theta bridges carry theta-side structure in the Hodge-theater taxonomy. They must be kept distinct from NF bridges and from theta links.

NF bridge

NF bridges carry number-field-side bridge structure. They participate in gluing operations but do not by themselves constitute theta evaluation.

Theta-plus-minus bridge

The additive theory introduces plus/minus theta bridges to manage the additive symmetry layer associated to labels and cusps.

Theta-ell bridge

Theta-ell bridges encode the elliptic/torsion side of the additive combinatorial Teichmuller theory in IUT I, Section 6.

Theta-plus-minus-ellNF theater

This theater combines additive theta-plus-minus, theta-ell, and NF data. It is the source object for the theta and log-theta constructions in IUT II and III.

Theta evaluation

Galois-theoretic theta evaluation reconstructs theta-value data from mono-theta and group-theoretic information. It is a source for the horizontal theta links.

Tempered Gaussian Frobenioids

Tempered Gaussian Frobenioids encode Gaussian theta-value distributions in a Frobenioid-theoretic setting before the global Gaussian layer.

Global Gaussian Frobenioids

Global Gaussian Frobenioids synchronize local Gaussian data and supply the realified Frobenioid objects used in theta-link comparisons.

Theta×μ link

The Theta×μ link is the full poly-isomorphism F×μ_env -> F×μ_△ of IUT II Corollary 4.10(iii). It is obtained by functorially upgrading a nonempty set of isomorphisms of F-prime-strips to isomorphisms of F×μ-prime-strips.

Theta×μ-gau link

The Gaussian version is the full poly-isomorphism F×μ_gau -> F×μ_△. Corollary 4.10 treats it parallel to the Theta×μ link, while Remark 4.10.2 explains why the later papers mainly use its enhanced descendants.

Coric F-prime-strip

Coric F⊢×μ-prime-strip data is invariant under both theta-link variants. Corollary 4.10(iv) identifies F⊢×μ_△ across theaters and then induces the corresponding D⊢_△ poly-isomorphism.

Multiradial theta monoids

IUT III Section 2 develops theta monoids whose constructions remain meaningful across the relevant alien holomorphic viewpoints.

Local holomorphic tensor packet

IUT III, Proposition 3.1 starts with an n-capsule of F-prime-strips. Over v_Q, the one-packet is the direct sum of the local logarithmic modules over all v | v_Q; the n-packet is their tensor product, equivalently a direct sum over all choices of one place in each tensor factor.

Local mono-analytic tensor packet

IUT III, Proposition 3.2 repeats the tensor-packet construction for D⊢-prime-strips, and when these arise from F⊢×μ-prime-strips it records the poly-isomorphisms to the holomorphic packets. The integral structures now come from mono-analytic log-shells.

Global tensor packet

IUT III, Proposition 3.3 defines (†M_mod)_A as a tensor product over α∈A of the labeled fields (†M_mod)_α for a subset of Hodge-theater labels and embeds it into the product of the local holomorphic tensor packets by localization composed with the given log-links.

Different estimates

IUT IV begins with estimates for differents in tensor products of local fields. These estimates feed the explicit log-volume bounds.

Normalized log-volume estimates

Nonarchimedean normalized log-volume estimates are the local numerical estimates that later combine into the theta-pilot log-volume theorem.

Prime number theorem input

IUT IV uses a prime-number-theorem estimate as part of the elementary analytic input for effective global inequalities.

Arithmetic divisors

Arithmetic divisors in IUT IV package the global arithmetic data whose degrees and heights enter the final Diophantine estimates.

Mutation history

A mutation history records a sequence of species-level transformations. It is the formal language for tracking inter-universal movement without collapsing implementations.

Sources: IUT I IUT II IUT III IUT IV Related: source library glossary

Anabelian Background

IUT is built on anabelian reconstruction: information about arithmetic curves is recovered from fundamental groups and related Galois-theoretic structures. Collas describes IUT as a continuation of Grothendieck's anabelian program through a new geometry of monoids; Mochizuki's gateway report frames IUT as a route from Diophantine geometry toward analytic number theory. Recent Collas papers also place this background in the active France-Japan anabelian and Galois-Teichmuller program.

Anabelian route from curves and covers to group-theoretic reconstruction
Source layer Anabelian object Role in this wiki
IUT I, Introduction and §1 Hyperbolic orbicurves, finite etale coverings, cusps, inertia groups, and tempered/etale fundamental groups. Explains why initial theta data includes orbicurve and covering data before Hodge theaters are built.
IUT I, Theorem B Group-theoretic recognition of decomposition and inertia data in tempered fundamental groups of p-adic hyperbolic curves. Source for reconstructing local valuation-indexed structure from group-theoretic data.
IUT I, etale-picture discussion Absolute anabelian recovery of ring/scheme structures from etale-like data, then deliberate use of that recovery inside alien copies. Prevents treating Hodge theaters as arbitrary records; their etale side is supposed to carry reconstruction algorithms.
IUT IV §3 and 2026 Lean report Species, mutations, and functorial algorithms as a language for reconstruction independent of one set-theoretic realization. Connects anabelian reconstruction to formalization interfaces and the LANA/RIMS Lean routes.
Collas overview, §2.2 Fukugen, decomposition/inertia reconstruction, Kummer reconstruction of multiplicative monoids, and additive recovery from valuations. Gives a reader-facing route from classical anabelian geometry to the monoid-transport mechanisms that IUT uses.
Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi 2025 Reconstruction from symmetries, from Galois theory and absolute Galois groups to etale fundamental groups and contemporary anabelian trends. Provides a compact non-IUT-specific entry point for why group-theoretic reconstruction is plausible and mathematically structured.

Classical anabelian input

Hyperbolic curves, their coverings, and their arithmetic fundamental groups are the base objects. IUT I uses this input when reconstructing local decomposition data, cusps, and valuation-indexed structures.

Combinatorial and mono-anabelian methods

Hoshi and Mochizuki's background work turns reconstruction into functorial algorithms. The wiki treats these algorithms as a prerequisite layer for Hodge theaters and prime-strips.

Geometry of monoids

Collas emphasizes IUT's monoid geometry as a way to move beyond direct ring/scheme comparison. This is exactly why the wiki keeps separate pages for Frobenioids, log-shells, and prime-strip interfaces.

Fukugen reconstruction

Collas presents anabelian geometry as reconstruction, or fukugen: recover a space from its etale fundamental group and the functorial symmetries encoded by covers. The IUT relevance is that reconstruction is algorithmic input, not merely a philosophical analogy.

Collas §2.2reconstruction

Two-step monoid recovery

In Collas's summary of absolute mono-anabelian reconstruction, one first reconstructs multiplicative data through Kummer-theoretic containers, then reconstructs additive structure by using valuation quotients of function fields. This separates the two monoid structures before recovering a field.

Collas §2.2.2Kummer

Mono-anabelian transport

Collas describes mono-anabelian transport as moving Frobenius-like monoid data through etale-like containers, subject to indeterminacies. This is the expository bridge from p-adic anabelian reconstruction to IUT's theta-link and log-link transport.

Collas §2.2.3transport

Second regeometrization

Collas frames IUT as a second regeometrization of arithmetic geometry: after anabelian geometry unified arithmetic and geometry through fundamental groups, IUT tries to include Diophantine estimates by dismantling and comparing additive and multiplicative monoid structures.

Collas §3IUT framing

Hodge-theater synchronization

Collas explains Hodge theaters as synchronizing geometric symmetries of the pointed elliptic curve side with arithmetic symmetries of the number-field side. This is the expository version of the theater as a typed environment for the later theta/log link operations.

Collas §3.1.1synchronization

Indeterminacies in the Collas map

Collas locates Ind1 in mono-analytic etale-like comparison, Ind2 in the etale-like versus Frobenius-like comparison, and Ind3 in the noncommutativity of log-Kummer correspondence. This matches the wiki's separation of indeterminacy pages from ordinary quotient-invariance language.

Collas §3.1.2Ind1/2/3

Reconstruction from symmetries

Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi introduce anabelian geometry through the question whether numbers and geometric spaces can be reconstructed from their symmetries. They start from Galois theory and then pass to absolute Galois groups and fundamental groups.

CMY 2025 §1symmetries

Homotopic arithmetic geometry

The same survey frames etale fundamental groups as the arithmetic-geometric analogue of topological loop groups. This is the broad background behind IUT's reliance on tempered and etale fundamental groups of hyperbolic curves and orbicurves.

CMY 2025 §2etale pi1

GT as anabelian container

Collas's 2026 Galois-Teichmuller paper places braid groups, mapping-class groups, and Grothendieck-Teichmuller symmetries inside an anabelian framework. The point for this wiki is not that GT proves IUT, but that it supplies a parallel model in which group-theoretic containers retain arithmetic information after geometric presentation data is removed.

Collas GT §3anabelian container

BGT models and G_Q

In Collas's account, Belyi-Grothendieck-Teichmuller models involve multiple ring structures connected by non-algebraic identifications. This is useful background for IUT because it normalizes the distinction between a shared abstract group-theoretic container and the particular arithmetic-holomorphic structures carried by its realizations.

Collas GT §4.2G_Q

Deligne-Mumford curve inertia

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi develop anabelian reconstruction for Deligne-Mumford curves. Their 2026 results treat stack inertia as information visible in etale fundamental groups, not as decoration added after reconstruction. This supports the wiki's treatment of orbicurves, cusps, and inertia as part of the input geometry.

CPY 2026 §1orbicurves

Three-step detection

The Deligne-Mumford curve paper proves detection results for hyperbolicity, affineness, and related inertia phenomena from low-level solvable quotients in its stated setting. The relevance here is methodological: some geometric predicates entering IUT-style orbicurve data can be formalized as group-theoretic recognizers.

CPY 2026 §2.4recognition

Affine stacky reconstruction

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi formulate a five-step anabelian reconstruction theorem for rigidifying affine stacky curves in their framework. This belongs in the IUT wiki as current evidence that the orbicurve side of the theory is an active formal reconstruction problem, separate from the later theta/log-volume comparison.

CPY 2026 §3rigidification

Gateway framing

Mochizuki's anabelian gateway report presents IUT as a bridge from Diophantine geometry to analytic number theory. The practical link inside the main series is through log-volume estimates.

N-th power monoid map

The Gateway report uses the N-th power map as an elementary model: it is an isomorphism of multiplicative monoids but not a ring homomorphism. This is the basic reason the common multiplicative interface cannot be read as equality of ring structures.

Gateway §2monoid not ring

Projective-line gluing analogy

The same report compares IUT gluing to constructing P1 by gluing two labeled affine lines along Gm using inversion. Deleting the labels turns a meaningful gluing into a contradiction unless one passes to a quotient or collection of possibilities.

Open concept page

Gateway §3.1labels

ZFCG and data types

Gateway §3.3 stresses that IUT is formulated in ZFC with Grothendieck universes and works with diagrams and species-like packages of data, not only with selected underlying sets. This supports the wiki's insistence on typed records and closed-loop comparisons.

Gateway §3.3species

Galois-orbit IUT

Section 4 describes a Galois-orbit version of IUT as work in progress, with claimed applications to local-global section-conjecture conditions, Siegel zeroes for imaginary-quadratic Dirichlet L-functions, and stronger abc/Szpiro inequalities.

Gateway §4work in progress

Formalization implication

Lean development should not treat anabelian reconstruction as a black box forever. It can start as typed interfaces, but the long-term project must replace those interfaces by constructions from anabelian source results.

Sources: Collas overview Collas 2026 CMY 2025 Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi 2026 Hoshi introduction Anabelian Gateway Anabelian Gateway 2025 Related: prime strips Hodge theaters LANA formalization

Collas Category View

Benjamin Collas presents IUT as a Grothendieck-style categorification of a Diophantine problem: the classical ring/scheme framework is not discarded, but it is reorganized through functorial anabelian reconstruction, monoid geometry, Frobenioids, Hodge theaters, species, and logical structure. This is an interpretive map for reading the source papers. Its key mathematical checkpoint is that IUT simulates a global multiplicative subspace that exists locally but typically not globally, while keeping track of operations that are not compatible with one fixed ring structure.

From schemes to category-theoretic containers Collas's expository route into IUT

Functorial unification

Collas frames Grothendieck's program as a functorial and category-theoretic reconciliation of discrete arithmetic and continuous geometry. In that setting, absolute Galois groups and etale path groups are treated as related manifestations of one reconstruction language.

Collas §1functoriality

Geometry of monoids

The Collas overview emphasizes IUT's shift beyond the direct geometry of rings and schemes toward additive and multiplicative monoid structures. The point is not to forget rings, but to separate the two operations so that non-ring-theoretic comparisons can be made explicit.

Collas §2.2monoids

Categorifying a Diophantine problem

Section 3.1 describes IUT as categorifying the problem of constructing a global multiplicative subspace for elliptic-curve height estimates. The category-theoretic simulation uses local multiplicative data, Frobenioid packages, and Hodge theaters instead of an actual global subspace in the general case.

Collas §3.1GMS simulation

Local existence, global failure

Collas isolates the first obstruction: the desired ell-torsion multiplicative subspace exists at suitable local places, but typically not as a single global subspace over the number field. Hodge theaters are the categorical replacement for this missing global object.

Collas §3.1local/global

Dilation is not ring-theoretic

The second obstruction is that the quotient/dilation operation behind the height estimate behaves like a Frobenius operation but is not a ring homomorphism. This is the source of the separation between multiplicative and additive monoid structures across theta links.

Collas §3.1ring break

Labels and basepoints are structural

In Collas's account, distinct but isomorphic copies, multiple basepoints, and log-shell containers are not presentation overhead. They record that theta and log links compare weakened structures without identifying their original ring histories.

Collas §3.1.1non-identification

Hodge theaters as containers

In Collas's reading, Hodge theaters synchronize geometric and arithmetic symmetries while keeping multiplicative and additive structures separated. This matches the wiki's treatment of theaters as typed containers rather than as ordinary copies of one scheme.

Collas §3.1.1containers

Log-theta wandering

Collas describes the log-theta lattice as a category-theoretic setting for relating distinct Frobenius-like objects by multiradial algorithms. This is the interpretive bridge from Hodge-theater construction to the Theorem 3.11 and Corollary 3.12 corridor.

Collas §3.1.2multiradial

Universes, species, and logic

Collas separates an external logical level, described in terms of AND/OR articulation, from an internal category-theoretic level involving objects, morphisms, functors, and Grothendieck universes. This supports the wiki's page model: concepts must retain their type and source context.

Collas §3.1.3species

Height comparison across universes

Collas stresses that local heights may live in distinct copies of the real numbers attached to distinct reconstruction contexts. The IUT comparison becomes meaningful only after the species/mutation and indeterminacy apparatus supplies a common comparison setting.

Collas §3.1.3heights

BGT combinatorial models

Collas's 2026 GT paper treats Belyi-Galois-Teichmuller diagrams as combinatorial anabelian containers from which field operations on realizations of Qbar are reconstructed.

Collas GT §4.2field reconstruction

Deligne-Mumford curves

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi extend the reconstruction viewpoint to stacky curves by tracking stack inertia, rigidification, and low-step solvable quotients of etale fundamental groups.

CPY 2026stack inertia

Status for IUT reading

The Collas corpus is best used as a category-theoretic orientation layer: it explains why IUT isolates containers, reconstructions, and species, but Mochizuki's IUT I-IV remain the source for formal definitions.

scopeorientation only
Collas checkpointCategory-theoretic readingWiki consequence
§3.1 IUT starts from a local/global obstruction: local ell-multiplicative subspaces exist, but the needed global multiplicative subspace generally does not. GMS/GCG motivation
§3.1 The quotient/dilation operation behind the height estimate is not a ring homomorphism, so the comparison cannot live inside one unchanged ring/scheme history. theta/log links
§3.1 The Diophantine problem is categorified because local multiplicative subspaces need not assemble globally, and the relevant ell-power operation is not a ring homomorphism. GMS/GCG motivation
§3.1.1 Hodge theaters synchronize geometric symmetries of the pointed curve with arithmetic symmetries of the base field. Hodge theaters
§3.1.1 The ThetaNF and Theta±ell parts separate multiplicative arithmetic and additive geometry; the theta link respects one side but not the full ring structure. theta/log links
§3.1.1 Log-shells act as common containers for handling multiplicative and additive monoid structures, while multiple basepoints are unavoidable. basepoints
§3.1.2 The multiradial algorithm constructs a common container for Frobenius-like data of theta-linked theaters; Collas identifies Ind3 as the essential indeterminacy. multiradiality
§3.1.3 Collas separates external logical articulation from the internal category-theoretic/species layer where objects, morphisms, functors, and universe changes are controlled. species and mutations
§3.2.1 The theta-pilot and q-pilot comparison is presented as a comparison of regions in log-shell tensor-product containers, after indeterminacies make the alien ring-structure data comparable. Theorem 1.10 route
Collas sourceCategory-theoretic contributionUse in this wiki
Anabelian arithmetic geometry and IUT Presents IUT as categorification of a Diophantine problem: Hodge theaters simulate the missing global multiplicative subspace, while theta/log links separate incompatible ring structures. Orientation for GMS/GCG, Hodge theaters, and log-theta lattice.
Anabelian perspectives in GT theory Develops the GT side as a passage from braid computations to functorial and algorithmic anabelian reconstruction, including BGT models of Qbar from Belyi diagrams. Context for GT species, anabelian background, and the wiki's insistence on reconstruction algorithms rather than labels.
Symmetries and anabelian geometry Gives a short route from symmetry groups to reconstruction of arithmetic and geometric spaces, with contemporary emphasis on canonicity and categorification. Background for anabelian reconstruction and for explaining why IUT depends on canonical, source-independent recovery procedures.
Anabelian geometry for Deligne-Mumford curves Extends reconstruction to stacky curves by combining stack inertia, rigidification, and low-step solvable quotients of fundamental groups. Current anabelian context for IUT's orbicurve/Hodge-theater background; not a replacement for the IUT proof corridor.
Scope. Collas's text is highly useful for orientation and for designing human-auditable Lean interfaces, but it does not replace the exact definitions in IUT I-IV. In the wiki, Collas pages should point back to the corresponding Mochizuki source objects whenever a construction becomes formal.
Wiki design consequence. Collas's category-theoretic reading supports treating IUT concepts as separate typed pages rather than as one narrative scroll. Anabelian reconstruction, monoid geometry, Hodge theaters, and species/logical structure are linked as containers and transitions; their source definitions remain on the Mochizuki pages where the formal data is introduced.
Sources: Collas overview Collas GT perspectives Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi 2026 Related: anabelian background Frobenioids Hodge theaters GMS/GCG motivation projective-line gluing logical structure

Collas GMS Categorification

Collas's IUT overview presents the first entrance to IUT as a Diophantine categorification problem. The motivating object is a global ell-torsion multiplicative subspace for an elliptic curve. Local versions exist at suitable places, but the global subspace needed for the desired height argument is not available in general. IUT replaces that absent global object by Hodge-theater and Frobenioid containers.

Local multiplicative data, categorical global simulation Collas §3.1 as a navigation layer for IUT I-IV
Collas pointTechnical readingWiki target
Local existence At suitable local fields, elliptic curves admit the relevant ell-torsion multiplicative subspaces. GMS/GCG motivation
Global failure The desired global multiplicative subspace is generally absent, so a direct scheme-theoretic proof route is unavailable. Hodge theaters
Categorification IUT simulates the missing global object by category-theoretic containers, especially Frobenioids and Hodge theaters. IUT I Frobenioid package
Formalization guardrail A Lean object should not merely name a global multiplicative subspace; it must distinguish local availability from the categorical substitute. four-triangle route
Use in this wiki. This page is interpretive. The exact objects to formalize remain Mochizuki's Hodge-theater, Frobenioid, prime-strip, and theta/log-link constructions, not Collas's expository shorthand.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1 IUT I IUT III Related: Collas category view Hodge synchronization log-theta wandering

Collas Hodge Synchronization

Collas describes a Hodge theater as a synchronization device. It combines the arithmetic symmetries of the base field with the geometric symmetries of a pointed genus-one curve. The result is not one ordinary scheme copied many times, but a typed container in which multiplicative and additive information can be separated before later comparison.

Theater as synchronized container arithmetic and geometric symmetries remain typed

ThetaNF part

In Collas's account, this part is arithmetic and multiplicative. It points back to the number-field and Frobenioid side of the Hodge theater.

Theta±ell part

This part carries the geometric and additive side, tied to the curve and to the covering/symmetry data used to synchronize conjugates.

Multiple basepoints

Collas stresses that IUT does not operate with one unified basepoint. The labels and basepoint choices record genuinely different ring histories.

Formalization consequence. The theater interface should carry synchronization data, labels, and side information. A record that only stores an undifferentiated "copy of a curve" misses the point of this layer.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1.1 IUT I Related: Hodge theaters Theta±ell bridges basepoints

Collas Log-Theta Wandering

Collas reads the log-theta lattice as the setting in which IUT relates distinct Frobenius-like objects by multiradial algorithms. The important point for this wiki is not the picture alone, but the audit discipline it suggests: movement through theta links, log links, Kummer correspondences, and indeterminacies must preserve the exact type of the data being compared.

From alien theaters to a common container Collas §3.1.2, aligned with IUT III Theorem 3.11
LayerRoleSource target
Theta link Moves between distinct Hodge-theater labels and is compatible with the multiplicative side, not with one fixed full ring structure. IUT I Corollary 3.7
Log link Relates multiplicative source data to additive target data through log-shell containers. IUT III 1.1
Kummer route Provides the etale-like access path between Frobenius-like data and the common container, subject to indeterminacies. Log-Kummer correspondences
Ind3 Collas identifies this as the essential indeterminacy for the main theorem and the height comparison application. Indeterminacies
3.12 relevance. This is the Collas-side explanation of why the wiki treats the Theorem 3.11 output as a typed multiradial construction, not as a bare equality between objects in one ambient scheme category.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1.2 IUT III Related: Theorem 3.11 algorithms Corollary 3.12 corridor multiradiality

Collas Universes and Species

Collas separates an external logical layer from an internal category-theoretic layer. The external layer concerns how statements are connected by AND/OR structure. The internal layer concerns categories, functors, objects, universes, species, and mutations. This distinction is useful for the wiki and for Lean work because the height comparison is not meant to be read inside one unchanged universe with one unchanged real line attached to all local data.

Logical structure and species discipline Collas §3.1.3 points back to IUT IV §3

External layer

Collas points to Mochizuki's logical analyses for the sequence of statements. The wiki uses this only as proof-route orientation.

Internal layer

Species and mutations are the IUT IV mechanism for controlling typed objects and transformations without identifying all outputs in one raw set.

Height comparison

Local heights may be attached to different reconstruction contexts. The common comparison setting is an output of the IUT apparatus, not a default.

Formalization consequence. This page supports keeping species, mutations, and comparison universes explicit. It also explains why the wiki links Collas's interpretation directly to IUT IV §3 rather than treating it as independent formal source material.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1.3 IUT IV §3 Related: species vs categories mutation histories/cores same-species parity

Collas Current Anabelian Work

Collas' recent papers are useful context for the wiki because they explain the anabelian and category-theoretic environment in which IUT and LANA sit. They should be read as orientation and adjacent reconstruction work: source definitions for IUT still come from Mochizuki's IUT I-IV and related official notes.

Current anabelian route from symmetry reconstruction to IUT context
PaperMain contentUse in this wiki
Symmetries of Spaces and Numbers Collas, Murotani, and Yamaguchi introduce anabelian geometry through the question of reconstructing numbers and spaces from their symmetries, moving from Galois theory to etale fundamental groups and current trends. Use as the lowest-barrier entry to the anabelian reconstruction idea behind IUT's source machinery.
Anabelian Perspectives in GT Theory Collas reviews the interface between braid/mapping-class groups, Galois-Teichmuller theory, moduli spaces of curves, Nakamura-style anabelian geometry, and combinatorial reconstruction of G_Q. Use to explain why GT and IUT are both discussed through algorithmic, group-theoretic reconstruction, while keeping their technical claims separate.
Anabelian Geometry for Deligne-Mumford Curves Collas, Philip, and Yamaguchi develop reconstruction results for Deligne-Mumford curves, including stack inertia, rigidification, coarsification, and low-step solvable quotient detection. Use as current background for orbicurve and stack-inertia themes relevant to anabelian formalization; do not read it as an IUT 3.12 proof input.

Algorithmic reconstruction

The common thread is not just group comparison, but reconstruction by explicit procedures. This matches the species/mutation emphasis in IUT IV and Mochizuki's formalization report.

Container caution

Collas' GT paper explicitly discusses group-theoretic containers. In this wiki, such containers are orientation tools unless the IUT source supplies the exact comparison and numerical bridge.

Formalization relevance

The Deligne-Mumford curve paper explains why stack inertia and low-step quotient information matter. This is adjacent to LANA's anabelian-library work before direct IUT formalization.

Sources: CMY 2025 Collas GT 2026 CPY 2026 Related: Collas category view anabelian background LANA species and mutations

GT Species Perspective

Mochizuki's October 2025 NGR talk notes, joint work in progress with Tsujimura, compare three Teichmuller-type situations: classical complex Teichmuller theory, IUT, and Grothendieck-Teichmuller theory. For this wiki the value is diagnostic: it explains IUT's multiradial representation as an arithmetic common-container analogue, while keeping the later GT statements separate and explicitly marked as expected where the source does so.

Three common-container patterns complex Teichmuller, IUT, and GT
Source sectionConceptWiki reading
§1 Two holomorphic structures on the same topological surface embed into the common sheaf of complex-valued real analytic functions. This is the classical model for a common container that is larger than either holomorphic structure.
§2 The N-th power map gives a multiplicative monoid isomorphism that is not compatible with addition and hence not a ring homomorphism. Mochizuki uses this as an elementary analogue for theta-link-type gluing: common multiplicative/coric data does not identify ring histories.
§2 IUT's multiradial representation is described as a common container for distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures, built using anabelian geometry, logarithms, theta-function Galois evaluation, and Kummer theory. This reinforces the wiki rule that multiradial comparison is not a direct ring isomorphism between the two sides of a theta link.
§3 The expected Theorem A states faithfulness of the natural outer action of GT on a genus-zero quotient of the geometric fundamental group of the tripod. This is a GT-side anabelian analogue, not an established input to the four IUT papers.
§4 The expected FICSP/COF route characterizes G_Q inside GT by synchronization of decomposition groups; the p-adic variant is stated as GT_p = G_Qp. The page records this as current adjacent research into anabelian/GT rigidity, with the source's expected-status caveat intact.
Varying Hues §3-4 Combinatorial algebraization theory uses idempotents in function algebras, Chebotarev/Hilbertian inputs, and combinatorial anabelian geometry to compare conjugates of arithmetic groups inside GT or Out(Pi_X). This extends the common-container pattern from IUT toward a GT-side Teichmuller theory; it is recorded as joint work in progress in the source.

Complex common container

Section 1 reviews the classical pattern O_H1 subset O_R superset O_H2: two holomorphic structures on one topological surface are compared inside a larger real analytic sheaf.

§1Teichmuller

N-th power gluing model

Section 2 uses the N-th power map on a multiplicative monoid as a simple non-ring-theoretic gluing. It is compatible with multiplicative data and group action, but not with addition.

§2monoid gluing

IUT multiradial container

The IUT paragraph identifies common multiplicative monoid and abstract group structures as the logical-AND input for the multiradial representation of the theta-pilot, with ring structures remaining distinct.

IUT §2multiradial

GT genus-zero quotient

The expected GT theorem concerns the action of GT on the genus-zero quotient Pi_X^0 attached to the tripod. The paper treats this through combinatorial anabelian geometry.

§3expected

CAT idempotent function theory

Varying Hues §3 states an idempotent lemma for subalgebras of function spaces on finite etale covers. After restricting to an infinite active subset, the nonzero idempotents form a maximal orthogonal family with transitive action by the cover's Galois group.

Varying Hues §3CAT

CAT miraculous rotation

Varying Hues §4 compares CAT with IUT's theta-link-to-log-link rotation: a mysterious gap between distinct ring theories attached to GT conjugates is rotated to a harmless gap between conjugates of finite characteristic quotients.

Varying Hues §4GT analogy

FICSP synchronization

The FICSP/COF condition asks whether restrictions of GT-decomposition groups to conjugates of an arithmetic Galois group are synchronized. Under this condition, the expected result characterizes G_Q inside GT.

§4decomposition groups

p-adic GT analogy

The notes state an expected p-adic analogue GT_p = G_Qp and a tempered absolute anabelian result for graphic cuspidalizable outer automorphisms. These are recorded as adjacent anabelian research, not as wiki prerequisites for IUT I-IV.

§4p-adic
Status caveat: the GT-side Theorem A, Theorem B, and Corollaries C-D are labelled "Expected" in the source. The wiki therefore treats this page as an explanatory and research-context bridge, not as a settled theorem page.
Sources: GT species paper Varying Hues Related: anabelian background species and mutations logical structure multiradiality

GT Common Containers

Mochizuki's 2025 GT notes compare three common-container patterns. Classical complex Teichmuller theory embeds two holomorphic structures into a real analytic sheaf. IUT builds a multiradial common container for theta-linked arithmetic holomorphic structures. The GT direction seeks function-space containers for distinct conjugates of G_Q inside GT.

Three container patterns Mochizuki-Tsujimura NGR2025 route
CaseContainerWarning
Complex Teichmuller Real analytic functions contain the two holomorphic sheaves. The container is larger than either holomorphic structure.
IUT Multiradial representation of the theta-pilot contains data from distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures. The source says the ring structures remain distinct.
GT Function spaces are recovered from decomposition groups and genus-zero data. The GT theorems in the note are marked expected.
Sources: GT species paper §§1-4 Related: GT species perspective Collas category view multiradiality

GT N-Power IUT Analogy

Section 2 of the GT notes gives an elementary arithmetic analogue for the theta-link situation. On the multiplicative monoid of an integral domain, the N-th power map is an isomorphism onto its image when viewed multiplicatively, but it is not compatible with addition and does not arise from a ring homomorphism. This is the source's simple model for non-ring-theoretic gluing.

Compatible with multiplication, not with addition the elementary model in GT species §2
Formalization warning. This example is useful exactly because the multiplicative comparison fails to be a ring map. A formal model that silently upgrades it to ring equality loses the source point.
Sources: GT species paper §2 Related: theta/Frobenius pictures theta links Collas GMS categorification

GT FICSP and Decomposition Groups

Sections 3 and 4 of the GT notes move from the IUT analogy to Grothendieck-Teichmuller theory. The expected Theorem A concerns faithfulness of the natural outer action of GT on a genus-zero quotient of the tripod fundamental group. The expected Theorem B uses a decomposition-group synchronization condition, FICSP/COF, to characterize G_Q inside GT.

Expected GT route genus-zero quotients, decomposition groups, synchronization
Source itemStatus in notesWiki handling
Theorem A Marked expected. Record as current GT research context, not as an IUT dependency.
FICSP/COF Condition used for Theorem B. Useful as a synchronization analogue for readers, but separate from 3.12.
Corollaries C-D Marked expected and p-adic/tempered. Adjacent anabelian research, not part of the four-paper proof spine.
Status caveat. This page preserves the source's expected labels. It should not be cited as a settled theorem page in the wiki.
Sources: GT species paper §§3-4 Related: GT species perspective anabelian background Collas current anabelian work

GMS/GCG Motivation

Mochizuki's survey and logical-structure notes use global multiplicative subspaces and global canonical generators as the elementary model for why IUT has to build Hodge theaters, theta links, log links, and multiradial representations. In the special case where a global multiplicative subspace exists, height control is visible through an isogeny; in the general case, IUT is described as simulating the missing GMS/GCG package.

From actual GMS to simulated GMS/GCG motivation for Hodge theaters and theta/log machinery
SourcePointWiki consequence
Mutually Alien Copies §2.3 A global multiplicative subspace is a rank-one l-torsion subspace defined globally and matching the multiplicative Tate-uniformization subspace at bad multiplicative places. It is a special extra structure, not generally available for arbitrary elliptic curves.
Mutually Alien Copies §2.3 Quotienting by such a subspace makes the new q-parameters behave like q -> q^l, while dlog(q) scales by l. This gives the prototype for inequalities of the form l*h <= h + C.
Logical Structure I-V §1 Mochizuki summarizes IUT as an attempt to simulate global multiplicative subspaces and global canonical generators by theta functions and anabelian geometry. The later Hodge-theater and theta-link layers should be read as replacing a missing global geometric convenience, not as arbitrary bookkeeping.
Anabelian Gateway 2025 §2 The N-th power map model separates common multiplicative/abstract-group data from incompatible ring structures. This explains why deleting labels too early turns a simultaneous AND relation into an unhelpful OR ambiguity.

Global multiplicative subspace

In the motivating special case, a rank-one subspace of E[l] is globally defined and agrees locally with the multiplicative subgroup from Tate uniformization at the bad multiplicative places.

Alien §2.3GMS

Global canonical generators

The logical-structure notes state that the theta-function route needs not only a GMS simulation but also global canonical generators. In IUT these are represented through label/cusp and theta-value machinery rather than a literal global section.

Logical I-V §1GCG

q-parameter l-th power

The quotient by an actual GMS changes bad-place q-parameters by an l-th power. Mochizuki repeatedly uses this as the prototype for the Frobenius-like dilation that IUT tries to simulate.

q -> q^lFrobenius-like

dlog height container

The differential relation dlog(q) -> l*dlog(q) supplies the common container in the special isogeny argument. In IUT the analogous role is played by logarithmic/anabelian machinery and log-shells.

dloglog link

Simulation problem

For arbitrary elliptic curves the required GMS generally does not exist. This absence is one reason IUT introduces distinct Hodge-theater copies, non-ring-theoretic theta links, log links, and multiradial algorithms.

general caseHodge theaters

Formalization obligation

A Lean model should not assume a hidden global multiplicative subspace in the general IUT case. The source route requires explicit replacement data: labels, theta values, Kummer comparison, log-shell containment, and multiradial invariance.

Leanno hidden GMS
Sources: Mutually Alien Copies Logical Structure I-V Anabelian Gateway 2025 Related: initial theta data Hodge theaters theta/log links multiradiality

Projective-Line Gluing Analogy

Mochizuki's 2025 Anabelian Gateway notes use the construction of P1 from two labeled affine lines as an elementary model for a recurring IUT warning: a gluing can be meaningful at one structural level while being incompatible with stronger ring-structure identifications. The page records this as an analogy for labels, indeterminacy, and multiradial transport, not as a proof of any IUT estimate.

Two charts, one glued object labels prevent false self-identification

Affine line as ring scheme

For a field or ring k, the affine line A1 = Spec(k[T]) carries its usual ring-scheme structure. Its open subscheme Gm = Spec(k[T,T^-1]) carries the multiplicative group-scheme structure of units.

Gateway 2025 §3A1/Gm

Inversion is only multiplicative here

Between labeled copies there is a group-scheme isomorphism dagger Gm -> double-dagger Gm sending dagger T to double-dagger T^-1. Mochizuki stresses that this does not extend to an isomorphism of the two affine-line ring schemes.

Gateway 2025 §3multiplicative not ring

P1 as two-chart gluing

The standard construction of P1 glues two affine-line charts along Gm by inversion. On the overlap, one rational function is read as dagger T from one chart and as double-dagger T^-1 from the other.

Gateway 2025 §3gluing

Why label deletion is dangerous

If the two labels are erased and both charts are treated as one copy of A1, the gluing forces the standard coordinate T to agree with T^-1. Mochizuki presents this as an elementary source of apparent consistency problems in noncommutative gluing diagrams.

Gateway 2025 §3labels

Riemann-sphere picture

Over C, the same gluing is pictured as the Riemann sphere assembled from northern and southern hemispheres along the equator. An oriented equatorial flow appears clockwise from one hemisphere and counterclockwise from the other.

Gateway 2025 §3orientation

Transport replaces naive identification

Rather than deleting hemisphere labels, the Gateway notes relate the two sides by metric/geodesic geometry. Mochizuki identifies this, at the level of analogy, with the anabelian-geometry-based multiradial algorithm for the theta-pilot.

Gateway 2025 §3multiradial analogy
Formalization use. This analogy is a guardrail for the wiki and Lean work: it supports explicit labels for copied structures, separate interfaces for multiplicative and ring data, and recorded indeterminacy when a comparison passes through a weaker common interface.
Sources: Anabelian Gateway 2025 Essential Logical Structure Mutually Alien Copies Related: GMS/GCG motivation mutually alien copies indeterminacies multiradiality GT species perspective

IUT I Gluing Models

IUT I Introduction §I2 explains how multiple theta-Hodge theaters are glued into a Frobenius-picture. Each theater is a miniature model of conventional scheme theory, but the theta-link between neighboring theaters is not a scheme-theoretic morphism: it is a Frobenioid/monoid-level gluing of theta-values to q-parameters. This page records the source analogies because they are a guardrail against modeling theta-links as ring homomorphisms.

Gluing miniature scheme worlds IUT I §I2 analogies for non-scheme-theoretic comparison

Miniature scheme-theory models

Mochizuki describes a theta-Hodge theater as a system of Frobenioids intended to function as a small model of conventional scheme theory. The Frobenius-picture is formed by gluing many such theaters {n HT} along theta-links.

IUT I §I2Hodge theaters

Theta-link outside schemes

The source text states that the essential correspondences n theta_v -> (n+1) q_v do not arise from ring homomorphisms. The relation between neighboring theaters therefore cannot be expressed as an ordinary scheme-theoretic relation.

IUT I §I2not ring maps

Topological surface analogy

IUT I compares conventional scheme theory to Euclidean space and the Frobenius-picture to a topological manifold built by gluing local Euclidean pieces. The figure in §I2 depicts twice-punctured genus-one surfaces glued along tubular neighborhoods of cycles.

IUT I Fig. I2.1analogy

Cantor-function alien ring example

The second analogy glues two real lines R0 and R1 by a bijection from the Cantor set C0 to the interval I1. Describing structures native to the alien ring structure of R0 using only the ring structure of R1 is presented as highly nontrivial.

IUT I §I2alien structure

Forgetting the integral monoid

For the pair G acts O-delta, IUT I recalls an absolute-anabelian result: forgetting O-delta gives a bijection on automorphism groups. In this case no extra unit-like ambiguity remains after forgetting the monoid.

AbsTopIII inputbijection

Forgetting the unit monoid

For G acts O^x, forgetting the unit monoid only gives a surjection on automorphism groups; the kernel is the natural Zhat^x-action on O^x. This is the closer model for why theta-link gluing retains a nontrivial ambiguity.

AbsTopIII inputsurjection

Kummer gluing cycle

Kummer theory supplies the precise specification of the gluing cycle in each theater. IUT I stresses that the same cycle receives quite different precise descriptions on the two sides of a theta-link, and that Galois-compatible rigidity leaves Kummer theory as the acceptable specification mechanism.

IUT I §I2Kummer

Formalization obligation

The wiki and Lean model should represent theta-links as typed cross-theater interfaces, not as hidden equality of ring or scheme histories. Any later Corollary 3.12 experiment must preserve this distinction when it moves from Frobenius-like to etale-like data.

Lean guardrailtyped histories
Scope. These examples are explanatory analogies from IUT I, not independent proofs of the later inequalities. Their role in the wiki is to fix the intended type discipline before the log-theta lattice and Corollary 3.12 pages reuse theta-link data.
Sources: IUT I Related: theta-link Hodge theaters projective-line gluing mutually alien copies theta/log links log-theta lattice Corollary 3.12 corridor

Basepoints and Inter-universality

IUT I Introduction §I3 explains why theta-links and log-links are called inter-universal. A non-ring/scheme-theoretic filter does not preserve the auxiliary basepoint and fiber-functor data used to construct an etale fundamental group. Across such a filter, the common object is only the underlying abstract topological group, so anabelian reconstruction and species-level type discipline become structural necessities.

What survives a non-ring filter IUT I §I3: from scheme histories to abstract topological groups

Non-ring filter

IUT I §I3 describes the theta-link, and later the log-link, as filters between distinct theaters that are not compatible with the respective ring structures. This is the concrete setting for "surgery on the number field."

IUT I §I3filter

Fundamental-group basepoint

Etale fundamental groups are constructed from a basepoint, equivalently a geometric point and fiber functor into separably closed field data. That construction depends on the surrounding scheme/ring structure.

IUT I §I3fiber functor

Abstract topological group interface

Since the non-ring filter is not a scheme morphism, ordinary functoriality of etale fundamental groups is unavailable. Mochizuki's point is that the shared type that still makes sense on both sides is the abstract topological group underlying the etale fundamental group.

IUT I §I3etale-like

Fiber functors and universes

The phrase inter-universal refers here to relating distinct set-theoretic universes associated with distinct fiber functors and basepoints on the two sides of a non-ring/scheme-theoretic filter.

IUT I §I3inter-universal

Absolute anabelian pressure

Once only the abstract topological group can be compared, the project is forced toward absolute anabelian geometry: understand which ring/scheme structures can be recovered from the topological group alone.

IUT I §I3reconstruction

Species type discipline

IUT I explicitly says this discussion depends on distinguishing different types of mathematical objects, and points forward to the language of species developed in IUT IV. This is why the wiki treats species as part of the core formalism rather than as later notation.

IUT I §I3species

Conjugate synchronization

The same source paragraph points to synchronization between conjugacy indeterminacies of distinct copies of local Galois groups. This is a basepoint-sensitive inter-universal phenomenon that later reappears in theta evaluation and log-Kummer transport.

IUT I §I3synchronization

Theta-function rigidity

IUT I also flags rigidity properties of the etale theta function as an inter-universal phenomenon needed in later papers. This connects §I3 to the Hodge-Arakelov evaluation and theta-value Kummer pages.

IUT I §I3theta rigidity

Formalization rule

A formal model should not identify etale fundamental groups by silently transporting basepoints through theta-links or log-links. It should expose the weakening to abstract topological groups and state which anabelian reconstruction theorem restores which structure.

Lean guardrailno hidden basepoints
Sources: IUT I Related: IUT I gluing models anabelian background theta±ell relay theta evaluation route species and mutations Collas category view

IUT I Teichmuller Analogy

IUT I Introduction §I4 explains the word "Teichmuller" in the theory's name. The analogy is not merely decorative: complex Teichmuller theory varies a holomorphic structure inside a fixed real-analytic container, while IUT varies an arithmetic holomorphic structure inside mono-analytic containers. The theta direction dilates value-group data; the log direction rotates the additive and multiplicative dimensions.

From complex dilation to log-theta lattice IUT I §I4: fixed containers, varied holomorphic structures

Complex template

IUT I recalls the classical deformation z = x + iy -> Kx + iy: one real-analytic dimension is dilated while the other is held fixed. The varying object is the holomorphic structure; the fixed object is the real-analytic container.

IUT I §I4complex template

Arithmetic combinatorial dimensions

Mochizuki interprets a number-field ring structure as one arithmetic holomorphic dimension made from two combinatorial dimensions: addition and multiplication. Locally, these are read through unit groups and value groups.

IUT I §I4+ / x

Theta dilation of value groups

The theta-link is described as a Teichmuller deformation relative to a theta factor: value-group data are dilated by theta/q data, while local unit groups remain fixed up to isomorphism. This is the source-level reason the wiki separates unit and value-group interfaces.

IUT I §I4theta-link

Theta and log dimensions

IUT I says the later log-theta lattice has two dimensions matching the two combinatorial dimensions of a ring. The theta direction is made of Frobenius-picture deformations; the log direction rotates the additive and multiplicative dimensions while the arithmetic holomorphic structure is held fixed.

IUT I §I4lattice

Canonical bound in a fixed container

The same section points forward to Theorem 3.11 and Corollary 3.12: a priori unbounded theta deformations should admit canonical bounds once described inside a fixed mono-analytic container.

IUT I §I43.11 / 3.12

p-adic Teichmuller correspondence

Figure I4.1 compares IUT with p-adic Teichmuller theory: number field with punctured elliptic curve corresponds to a hyperbolic curve with nilpotent ordinary indigenous bundle; the log-theta lattice corresponds to canonical lifting data equipped with Frobenius action.

IUT I Fig. I4.1p-adic analogy

Witt-vector arrow analogy

IUT I identifies theta arrows with the mixed-characteristic extension structure of a ring of Witt vectors, and log arrows with the Frobenius morphism in positive characteristic. Their failure to commute models the noncommutativity of theta and log links.

IUT I §I4Witt/Frobenius

Anabelian reconstruction contrast

Mochizuki contrasts the relatively straightforward construction of canonical liftings with their nontrivial absolute-anabelian reconstruction. This mirrors IUT: constructing the log-theta lattice is not the hard part; describing an alien arithmetic holomorphic structure is.

IUT I §I4reconstruction

Formalization rule

A formal model should keep separate the fixed mono-analytic container, the arithmetic holomorphic structures being deformed, and the numerical bound extracted from the container. This is the type-level analogue of the complex Teichmuller fixed-container/varying-structure pattern.

Lean guardrailcontainer vs structure
Sources: IUT I Related: classical roots route Invitation deformation log-theta lattice theta/log links tensor/log-volume corridor Corollary 3.12 corridor

CAT Idempotent Function Theory

In Varying Hues §3, Mochizuki states an elementary idempotent lemma for combinatorial algebraization theory. Starting from a finite etale Galois cover and several algebra copies inside a function algebra on an infinite active point set, one may restrict to an infinite subset so that the nonzero idempotents form a maximal orthogonal family and the Galois group acts transitively on them.

Idempotents after restricting the active set Varying Hues §3, source lemma for CAT
Lemma componentContentCAT use
Finite etale Galois cover Provides the finite group Q acting on the function space. Finite quotient side of the later CAT rotation.
Active infinite subset The construction may restrict to an infinite subset after excluding finitely many points. Stabilizes the idempotent pattern used for algebraization.
Maximal orthogonal idempotents Nonzero idempotents correspond to connected components after restriction. Converts algebra copies into combinatorial component data.
Transitive action Q acts transitively; supports look like cosets qHy. Feeds the finite-quotient side of the CAT comparison.
Status. This page records a lemma from an ICMS 2025 talk note connected to manuscripts in preparation. It is current adjacent source material, not a dependency of the published IUT I-IV proof.
Sources: Varying Hues §3 Related: CAT miraculous rotation GT common containers GT species perspective

CAT Miraculous Rotation

Varying Hues §4 presents combinatorial algebraization theory as another Teichmuller-type theory, now for conjugates of arithmetic groups inside GT or Out(Pi_X). The source compares CAT's rotation from a mysterious gap between distinct ring theories to a harmless finite-quotient gap with IUT's rotation from the theta-link gap to the log-link gap.

CAT rotation compared with IUT rotation Varying Hues §4

CAT input

The source studies conjugates of G_Q or mapping-class-group images inside GT or Out(Pi_X).

Technical tools

Chebotarev and Hilbertian inputs compute tensor-product actions at decomposition subgroups; combinatorial anabelian geometry supplies the configuration-space transition.

IUT analogy

The analogy is structural: CAT rotates a difficult GT-conjugate gap to a finite-quotient gap, while IUT rotates the theta-link gap to the log-link gap.

Wiki handling. CAT is a useful comparison lens for the common-container theme. The page should remain linked to GT context and not be folded into the core IUT I-IV proof spine.
Sources: Varying Hues §4 Related: CAT idempotents GT FICSP/decomposition Collas log-theta wandering

Hoshi Introduction Route

Hoshi's Japanese introduction is organized as a pedagogical route into IUT: first cyclotomes and object types, then the link-based strategy for Diophantine inequalities, then local/additive machinery, then global Frobenioid and multiplicative Hodge-theater machinery. The wiki uses it as a reader's map of dependencies, not as a substitute for the four IUT papers.

Survey route from cyclotomes to full Hodge theaters

Cyclotomes first

Hoshi begins with cyclotomes because IUT repeatedly needs to compare cyclotomic structures arising from different sources: roots of unity, curve cohomology, inertia at cusps, and Frobenioid monoids. The survey presents cyclotomic synchronization and rigidity as a prerequisite for later transport.

Hoshi §1cyclotomes

Types of objects

The early sections distinguish etale-like parts from Frobenius-like parts. In the local model, the Galois group is the symmetry-bearing etale side, while monoids such as local units or integral multiplicative monoids form the quantity-bearing Frobenius side.

Hoshi §2etale/Frobenius

Kummer transport

Hoshi's local toy model explains Kummer theory as a way to transport a Frobenius-like monoid through etale containers. The inevitable ambiguity is split into Kummer-detachment indeterminacy and etale-transport indeterminacy.

Hoshi §3transport

Link approach

Sections 4-12 describe the strategy for obtaining Diophantine consequences through links: protect q-parameter and field data from destructive identifications by handling them as special values inside appropriate containers rather than as bare numbers in one fixed ring.

Hoshi §§4-12links

Core objects

The survey frames core objects as the portions that remain usable across transport. This matches the wiki's distinction between the data retained by prime-strips, log-shells, and Hodge theaters, and the ring/scheme histories deliberately not transported as-is.

Hoshi §§5-6cores

Multiradial algorithms

Hoshi isolates multiradiality before the technical Hodge-theater construction. In this wiki, that order is important: multiradial algorithms are the formal reason output may be read from many theater positions after allowing the specified indeterminacies.

Hoshi §7multiradiality

Hulls and log links

The middle survey route introduces hulls and log links before the full global setup. Hulls absorb indeterminacy into controlled regions; log links provide the vertical movement later visible in the log-theta lattice.

Hoshi §§8-9hulls/log links

Mild indeterminacies

Hoshi's route explicitly pauses on the "mild" indeterminacies before numerical output. This is a useful reading warning: Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3 are not cleanup details after the comparison, but part of the comparison interface itself.

Hoshi §10Ind1/2/3

Additive and multiplicative theaters

The later sections split the construction into additive Hodge theaters built from local/log-theta data and multiplicative Hodge theaters built from global Frobenioid and Kummer data. Section 26 explains the combined Hodge theater as the interface between these two symmetry packages.

Hoshi §§13-26Hodge theaters
Sources: Hoshi introduction Related: theta cyclotome theta/log links Hodge theaters indeterminacies

Hoshi Cyclotome Synchronization

Hoshi begins his introduction with cyclotomes because IUT repeatedly compares Tate-twist objects that arise from different mathematical sources. The same symbol Zhat(1) may be represented by roots of unity, by curve cohomology, by cusp inertia, or by Frobenioid data. The mathematical work is to control when these presentations may be synchronized.

One cyclotomic type, several sources Hoshi §1, used as prerequisite for later transport
Source presentationWhy it mattersWiki link
Roots of unity Provides the elementary Tate-twist model used in local field and Kummer settings. theta cyclotome
Curve cohomology Connects cyclotomic orientation to degree and Chern-class conventions. notation primitives
Cusp inertia Supplies a group-theoretic cyclotomic source in the anabelian setting. cusp recognizers
Frobenioid monoids Keeps multiplicative data compatible with the categorical line-bundle package. Frobenioid package
Reader warning. Hoshi's first section is not preliminary notation only. It is a guardrail against silently identifying cyclotomic structures that arise from different containers.
Sources: Hoshi introduction §1 Related: Hoshi route Kummer transport Kummer-ready/blind

Hoshi Kummer Transport

Hoshi's early local model presents Kummer theory as a transport mechanism: Frobenius-like monoid data can be moved through etale-like containers only after the cyclotomic comparison has been fixed. The result is never a raw equality of ring histories. It is a controlled passage with specified indeterminacy.

Etale container, Frobenius-like payload Hoshi §3 before the full Hodge-theater setting

Etale-like carrier

The Galois or fundamental-group side carries symmetry information and permits transport without preserving a chosen ring structure.

Frobenius-like payload

Unit monoids and integral monoids carry quantity data. Kummer maps expose them to the etale carrier but do not turn them into scheme-identical data.

Indeterminacy is part of transport

The local toy model already shows why later IUT comparisons must track indeterminacies as typed structure, not as post-proof noise.

Sources: Hoshi introduction §3 Related: cyclotomes Log-Kummer correspondences indeterminacies

Hoshi Link and Indeterminacy Route

Hoshi's sections 4 through 12 explain the strategic middle of IUT before the full Hodge-theater construction. The aim is to protect q-parameter data, field data, and hull/log-link containers while moving through links. The indeterminacies are introduced before the numerical output because they are necessary to state what remains comparable.

From link strategy to controlled output Hoshi §§4-12 as the pedagogical middle route
Survey section groupRole in the routeDetailed wiki target
§§4-6 Set up the link approach and isolate core objects that survive transport. base bridges/evaluation sections
§7 Introduces multiradial algorithms before the full technical theater setup. multiradiality
§§8-9 Introduce hulls and log links as controlled containers and movements. theta/log links
§10 Explains mild indeterminacies before numerical and Hodge-theater output. indeterminacies
3.12 relevance. This Hoshi route supports the wiki's main warning about the disputed corridor: the q-pilot and theta-pilot comparison is not a comparison of bare numbers before the link, hull, and indeterminacy apparatus is applied.
Sources: Hoshi introduction §§4-12 Related: Hoshi route Collas log-theta wandering Corollary 3.12 corridor

IUT I Notation Primitives

IUT I Section 0 fixes conventions that later pages use constantly. These are not optional stylistic choices: poly-isomorphisms, capsules, pseudo-monoids, and connected temperoids determine what kind of comparison is being made when a theorem says "isomorphism" or "full poly-isomorphism."

Conventions before the construction IUT I §0: monoids, categories, temperoids, valuations

Pseudo-monoid
