Remark 4.6.1 At archimedean places the distinguished non-unit element is not recovered from the local F-side mono-analytic object alone; compatible global Frobenioids act as currency-exchange devices across local divisor monoids. The wiki records this as a genuine global-dependence point, not a local reconstruction theorem.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.6 and Remarks 4.6.1-4.6.2 Related: D-side synchronization/evaluation Theta±ell Gaussian lift atlas ThetaNF Fl symmetry

Theta±ell Gaussian Lift in IUT II 4.6

Corollary 4.6 transfers the D-side global Gaussian construction of Corollary 4.5 to a Theta±ell-Hodge theater. The input is an F-prime-strip whose associated D-prime-strip is the one used in Corollary 4.5. Kummer-theoretic identifications lift constant monoids, semi-simplifications, label synchronization, local theta/Gaussian assignments, and global realified Frobenioids from the D-side to the F-side.

Frobenioid lift of global Gaussian data Corollary 4.6 over the Corollary 4.5 D-side construction
Source itemF-side constructionWhy it matters
Corollary 4.6(i) From an F-prime-strip F, construct Psi_cns(F) and a Kummer-theoretic collection of isomorphisms Psi_cns(F) -> Psi_cns(D). This is the constant-monoid lift from D-data to Frobenioid-theoretic F-data.
Corollary 4.6(ii) From the mono-analytic F-prime-strip F⊢, construct semi-simplifications and isomorphisms to the D-side semi-simplifications. The global F-prime-strip supplies a Frobenioid C isomorphic to D(D⊢). This identifies the global realified F-side degree object with the D-side realified container.
Corollary 4.6(iii) The isomorphisms of constant monoids produce labeled isomorphisms Psi_cns(F≻)_t -> Psi_cns(D≻)_t, with nonarchimedean conjugacy indeterminacy independent of the label, and compatible F_l^±-symmetrizing isomorphisms. This lifts the global conjugate synchronization of Corollary 4.5 to the F-prime-strip setting.
Corollary 4.6(iv) A theta bridge glued to the Theta±-bridge constructs Psi_Fenv, Psi_Fgau, and infinity variants, together with evaluation isomorphisms through the D-side Psi_env(D>) and Psi_gau(D>). This is the local F-theoretic theta/Gaussian assignment used by the theta-link side.
Corollary 4.6(v) Applying the F/D isomorphism to Denv and Dgau constructs global Frobenioids Cenv and Cgau, with evaluation isomorphisms Cenv -> Denv -> Dgau -> Cgau. This conjugates the D-side global formal evaluation into the F-side Frobenioid-theoretic world.
Remark 4.6.1 At nonarchimedean places, the semi-simplification isomorphism is algorithmically reconstructed from F⊢_v. At archimedean places the distinguished non-unit element is not reconstructed from F⊢_v alone; the global Frobenioids act as currency-exchange devices between local divisor monoids. The archimedean step needs the global object, not just local copying.

F-prime-strip constant monoids

Corollary 4.6(i) constructs constant monoids directly from an F-prime-strip and compares them to the associated D-prime-strip constant monoids through Kummer-theoretic isomorphisms.

Cor. 4.6(i)F-prime-strip

F⊢ semi-simplification

The F⊢-prime-strip supplies semi-simplified constant data with splittings. Corollary 4.6(ii) matches this data with the D⊢ semi-simplification and the global realified Frobenioid.

Cor. 4.6(ii)mono-analytic

F_l± synchronization on F-data

Corollary 4.6(iii) lifts the label-synchronized F_l^± symmetry from D-data to F-data, with the nonarchimedean conjugacy ambiguity independent of the label.

Cor. 4.6(iii)labels

Theta-bridge F-Gaussian assignments

The glued theta bridge constructs Psi_Fenv(HT_Theta) and Psi_Fgau(HT_Theta), together with local evaluation chains through the D-side monoids.

Cor. 4.6(iv)Theta bridge

Cenv and Cgau

Cenv and Cgau are the F-side global realified theta and Gaussian Frobenioids. They are obtained by conjugating Denv and Dgau through the F/D Frobenioid isomorphism.

Cor. 4.6(v)global

Global currency exchange

Remark 4.6.1 describes global Frobenioids as devices for translating between local divisor-monoid currencies. This is necessary at archimedean places where the local F⊢ data alone does not reconstruct the distinguished non-unit element.

Rem. 4.6.1archimedean
Formalization warning: Corollary 4.6 is not just another copy of Corollary 4.5. It records the Kummer-theoretic transport from D-prime-strip data to F-prime-strip/Frobenioid data and exposes the archimedean dependence on the global realified Frobenioid.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.6 Related: global Gaussian Frobenioids Theta±ell bridges ThetaNF Frobenioid lift Frobenioid prime-strips

IUT II Corollary 4.7(i): D-ThetaNF Non-Realified Global Structures

Corollary 4.7 begins the ThetaNF passage by reconstructing non-realified global structures from the D-ThetaNF side: global monoids and fields, invariant subobjects, Frobenioids, and the realification functor. This is the global base-field layer that only makes sense after isolating the F_l-symmetry context.

D-ThetaNF global base-field package Corollary 4.7(i), Remarks 4.7.6-4.7.7
ObjectConstructionRole
Global monoids and fields Constructs M(D), M(D), and M^infty-kappa(D), equipped with the relevant fundamental-group actions and conjugacy indeterminacies. Provides the non-realified global arithmetic data needed by the ThetaNF side.
Invariant subobjects Extracts Mmod, Msol, and Mkappa layers, including kappa-sol and infty-kappa versions. These layers let the later F_l-symmetric argument avoid non-synchronizable conjugacy at raw labels.
Frobenioids Constructs Fmod(D), F(D), and the realification Fmod(D) -> Fmod^R(D). Remark 4.7.6 says non-realified global Frobenioids belong naturally to the F_l-symmetry context.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.7(i) and Remarks 4.7.6-4.7.7 Related: ThetaNF Fl symmetry atlas Fl symmetry indeterminacy ThetaNF F-side localization

IUT II Corollary 4.7(ii) and Remarks 4.7.2-4.7.5: Fl Symmetry and Indeterminacy

The F_l-symmetry of the ThetaNF side is not the conjugate-synchronizing F_l^±-symmetry. It separates the zero label from the nonzero labels, supports the internal Gaussian-distribution structure, and carries independent conjugacy indeterminacies at distinct labels.

Fl symmetry is multiradial, not conjugate-synchronizing Corollary 4.7(ii), Remarks 4.7.2-4.7.5
SourceClaimConsequence
4.7(ii) F_l-symmetry acts on labeled F-prime-strips, mod/sol/kappa-sol objects, and realified global Frobenioids. Diagonal objects are formal shorthand for these symmetrizing isomorphisms. The construction avoids crushing labels while still packaging multiplicative symmetry.
Remark 4.7.2 Unlike F_l^±-symmetry, F_l-symmetry arises through the arithmetic portion of global fundamental groups and does not establish conjugate synchronization. This is why Corollary 4.7 works with F-prime-strips and mod/sol layers rather than raw Galois-action monoids.
Remarks 4.7.3-4.7.5 F_l^± corresponds to the additive/unit dimension and includes the zero label; F_l corresponds to the multiplicative/value dimension and separates zero from nonzero labels. The F_l side is multiradial and suited to the internal Gaussian distribution, while F_l^± is the single-basepoint synchronization side.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.7(ii) and Remarks 4.7.2-4.7.5 Related: D-ThetaNF non-realified Theta±ell F-side lift ThetaNF F-side localization

IUT II Corollary 4.8: ThetaNF F-Side Kummer and Localization

Corollary 4.8 lifts the D-ThetaNF construction to a ThetaNF-Hodge theater. Kummer isomorphisms compare F-side pseudo-monoids and Frobenioids with the D-side objects, while localization functors relate global realified Frobenioids to label-indexed local F-prime-strips.

ThetaNF F-side lift of Fl-localized data Corollary 4.8 and Remark 4.8.1
ClauseConstructionFunction
4.8(i) Kummer isomorphisms identify F-side pseudo-monoids, mod/sol layers, and Frobenioids with the D-side global structures of Corollary 4.7. Lifts the non-realified ThetaNF base-field apparatus from D-side to F-side.
4.8(ii) Label-indexed comparisons identify F_j, F|j, and F(D)|j, with the same F_l-symmetrizing and conjugacy-indeterminacy conventions as Corollary 4.7. Preserves the multiplicative label symmetry without claiming conjugate synchronization.
4.8(iii) Constructs compatible localization functors and an isomorphism C_j ~= Fmod^R_j, compatible with primes, local monoids, and Kummer comparisons. Supplies the local realified F-side containers used by the later Gaussian embeddings.
Remark 4.8.1 The global Cgau of Corollary 4.6 can be viewed inside the product of Fmod^R_j, and the local realified Fgau data embeds into the product of the corresponding local realified F-side pieces. Makes explicit how global Gaussian objects localize into the F_l-indexed ThetaNF framework.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.8 and Remark 4.8.1 Related: Fl symmetry indeterminacy ThetaNF localization atlas F-times-mu Kummer prime-strips

ThetaNF Fl Symmetry in IUT II 4.7

Corollary 4.7 studies the D-ThetaNF side glued to the D-Theta±ell side. It constructs non-realified global objects, labels them by F_l, forms formal diagonal structures, and then localizes them. The accompanying remarks stress the source distinction: unlike F_l^±, the F_l symmetry does not support conjugate synchronization, but it does support the multiplicative/multiradial structure needed for Gaussian distributions.

ThetaNF and Fl-symmetry multiplicative symmetry, formal diagonals, and localization
Source itemConstructionInterpretation
Corollary 4.7(i) From the category D, construct the morphism D -> D, global monoids/fields/pseudo-monoids M, Mmod, Msol, Mκ, M∞κ, and Frobenioids Fmod, F, plus the realification functor. This is the non-realified global arithmetic side; it only appears in the F_l-symmetry context.
Corollary 4.7(ii) A label j gives an F-prime-strip F(D)|j. The F_l action induces symmetrizing isomorphisms and formal diagonal F-prime-strips, submonoids, subrings, pseudo-monoids, and subcategories. These diagonals are formal shorthand for the symmetrizing isomorphisms, not synchronized diagonal Galois data.
Corollary 4.7(iii) Localization functors and poly-morphisms map global model data to F(D)|j and to local ∞κ-coric data. Realified global structures identify D(D⊢_j) with Fmod(D)^R_j. This is the passage from global ThetaNF data to the local F-prime-strip data used downstream.
Remark 4.7.2 Unlike F_l^±, the F_l symmetry arises from the arithmetic portion of global arithmetic fundamental groups. Therefore it does not establish conjugate synchronization. This forces the use of F-prime-strips and mod/sol/kappa objects rather than raw ind-topological monoids with synchronized Galois actions.
Remarks 4.7.3-4.7.5 F_l^± represents the additive dimension and includes the zero label; F_l represents the multiplicative dimension and separates zero from nonzero labels. The nonzero labels carry weight 1/l in weighted volume computation. This is the source explanation for why additive and multiplicative label symmetries cannot be merged.
Remark 4.7.6 Non-realified global Frobenioids make sense only in the F_l-symmetry context. Quotienting by the F_l indeterminacy would crush labels and destroy the Gaussian construction. Label preservation is a structural requirement, not presentational bookkeeping.

Non-realified global structures

Corollary 4.7(i) constructs the model field and Frobenioid side, including Fmod, before realification. This is possible in the F_l-symmetry setting.

Cor. 4.7(i)Fmod

Fl labels

A cusp label j determines an F-prime-strip F(D)|j. The F_l action moves between labeled objects but introduces independent conjugacy indeterminacies.

Cor. 4.7(ii)labels

Formal diagonal Fl data

The diagonal subobjects in Corollary 4.7(ii) are explicitly formal shorthand for the F_l-symmetrizing isomorphisms. They should not be read as a conjugate-synchronized diagonal.

Cor. 4.7(ii)formal

Localization

Corollary 4.7(iii) localizes global model Frobenioids and ∞κ-coric structures to the labeled local F-prime-strip data.

Cor. 4.7(iii)localization

Fl without conjugate synchronization

Remark 4.7.2 says F_l-symmetry cannot establish the conjugate synchronization of Corollary 4.5 because it arises from arithmetic Galois conjugacy.

Rem. 4.7.2no sync

F_l± versus Fl

Remark 4.7.3 identifies F_l^± with the additive side and F_l with the multiplicative side. The former includes zero and is suited to units; the latter separates zero from nonzero labels.

Rem. 4.7.3two dimensions

Multiradial Fl combinatorics

Remark 4.7.4 models F_l-actions on distinct spokes as commuting and compatible with spoke permutations, a prototype of multiradiality.

Rem. 4.7.4multiradial

Label-crushing warning

Remark 4.7.6 says one cannot quotient by the F_l indeterminacy, since that would identify distinct labels and destroy the Gaussian monoid construction.

Rem. 4.7.6labels

Mod objects as translation apparatus

Remark 4.7.2 says the ring structure on the mod-subscripted objects will later translate between additive and multiplicative interpretations of arithmetic line bundles.

Rem. 4.7.2IUT III input
Source warning: F_l^± and F_l are not interchangeable symmetry notations. The former is tied to single-basepoint conjugate synchronization and units; the latter is tied to multiplicative label separation and multiradial Gaussian structure.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.7 and Remarks 4.7.1-4.7.7 Related: global Gaussian Frobenioids Theta±ell Gaussian lift Theta±ellNF multiradiality

ThetaNF Frobenioid Localization in IUT II 4.8

Corollary 4.8 moves the F_l-labelled D-ThetaNF structures of Corollary 4.7 into a ΘNF-Hodge theater and records the Frobenioid-theoretic localization mechanisms that later feed the log-wall. Its new point is not a new Gaussian value formula: it is the compatible passage from non-realified global Frobenioids and F_l-labels to realified local Gaussian Frobenioids, including the translation apparatus for log-shifted units.

ThetaNF Frobenioid localization global non-realified data, labelled Frobenioids, and realified localization
Source itemConstructionRole in the IUT II-to-III corridor
Corollary 4.8(i) A functorial algorithm in the Frobenioid-theoretic ΘNF side constructs Kummer isomorphisms for global pseudo-monoids and interprets them as compatible isomorphisms of Frobenioids, including realified Fmod variants. This transports the non-realified global structures of Corollary 4.7 into the ΘNF-Hodge theater.
Corollary 4.8(ii) For each cusp label j, the isomorphisms identify the labelled F-prime-strip with the corresponding D-side construction and produce F_l- symmetrizing isomorphisms and formal diagonal subobjects. The label data remains subject to the independent conjugacy indeterminacies already present in Corollary 4.7.
Corollary 4.8(iii) The NF-bridge constructs localization functors/poly-morphisms from global model Frobenioids to labelled local F-prime-strips, together with a natural isomorphism C_j ≃ Fmod^R_j. This is the formal route from global ΘNF Frobenioids to realified local structures compatible with the Kummer identifications.
Remark 4.8.1 The global Gaussian Frobenioid Cgau is regarded inside the product of the Fmod^R_j, while the local Gaussian F-prime-strip Fgau^R embeds in the product of the F_j^R. These product embeddings induce the realified localization functor Cgau -> Fgau^R.
Remark 4.8.2 The realified localization concerns the Frobenioid-theoretic Gaussian side. Units are later used through their logarithms, and non-realified Frobenioids act as translation apparatus between multiplicative line-bundle data and additive log-images. This points forward to the IUT III log-wall and explains why the natural splittings of Gaussian monoids matter.
Remark 4.8.3 The same compatibility considerations as for F_l^±-symmetrizing isomorphisms apply to the F_l-symmetrizing isomorphisms of Corollary 4.8. This keeps the localization picture aligned with the previously fixed label symmetries.

Global Kummer isomorphisms

Corollary 4.8(i) constructs Kummer isomorphisms for ∞κ, κ, sol, and mod pseudo-monoid data, then reads them Frobenioid-theoretically.

Cor. 4.8(i)Kummer

Fl-labelled F-data

Each label j receives a compatible labelled F-prime-strip and corresponding mod, sol, and ∞κ structures.

Cor. 4.8(ii)labels

Localization functors

The NF-bridge gives functors and poly-morphisms from global model data to labelled local data, compatible with Corollary 4.7(iii).

Cor. 4.8(iii)NF bridge

C_j to Fmod^R_j

Corollary 4.8(iii) identifies the realified global Frobenioid piece C_j with Fmod^R_j, compatible with primes and local topological monoids.

Cor. 4.8(iii)realified

Cgau product embedding

Remark 4.8.1 regards Cgau as a subcategory of the product over j ∈ F_l of the realified Fmod_j pieces.

Rem. 4.8.1Cgau

Fgau^R product embedding

The local Gaussian F-prime-strip Fgau^R embeds, place by place, into the product of the labelled realified Frobenioids F_j^R.

Rem. 4.8.1local

Realified localization functor

Combining the two product embeddings yields Cgau -> Fgau^R, compatible with the local realified localization isomorphisms of Corollary 4.6(v).

Rem. 4.8.1localization

Log-shifted units

Remark 4.8.2 says the unit part of the Gaussian monoids will be used after applying log, while value-group data remains non-log-shifted.

Rem. 4.8.2log-wall

Non-realified translation apparatus

The non-realified global Frobenioids function as translation apparatus between multiplicative line-bundle data and additive logarithmic images of units.

Rem. 4.8.2IUT III
Source warning: Corollary 4.8 keeps the F_l label structure active. The realified localization functor is obtained after embedding both global and local Gaussian Frobenioids into labelled products; it is not a quotient that forgets the labels.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.8 and Remarks 4.8.1-4.8.3 Related: ThetaNF Fl symmetry Theta±ell Gaussian lift global Gaussian Frobenioids theta/log links log-theta lattice

F×μ Kummer Prime-Strips in IUT II 4.9

Definition 4.9 creates the weakened Kummer interfaces used by the modified theta links. It starts from a Frobenioid over the category of connected finite etale covers of a local or archimedean field, extracts unit and torsion-quotient data at a universal covering pro-object, and packages the result into F⊢×, F⊢×μ, F⊢ ×μ, and F×μ prime-strips.

Definition 4.9 construction route unit quotients and place-sensitive weakened prime-strips
Source itemDefinitionFormal meaning
Definition 4.9(i) For a Frobenioid C with universal covering pro-object A and G = Aut(A), define ×- and ×μ-Kummer structures as orbits of isomorphisms from group-theoretically reconstructed units to units evaluated at A. This is the unit-side interface. The × structure is unique; the ×μ structure is determined by the invariant submodules I_H^κ(A) for open subgroups H ⊆ G.
Definition 4.9(ii)-(iii) At bad nonarchimedean places, the 2l-torsion and splitting images generate O_perp(A). The quotient by μ_2l(A), together with units modulo torsion, builds a split ×μ-Kummer Frobenioid. The bad-place construction is where the theta-level 2l-torsion enters the weakened link domain.
Definition 4.9(iv) At good nonarchimedean places, the splitting image alone determines O_perp(A). The same unit-quotient mechanism produces the split ×μ-Kummer Frobenioid. Good places have no theta 2l-torsion generator in this definition; the source is the splitting image.
Definition 4.9(v) At archimedean places, replace the split Frobenioid by an inductive system of quotients by N-torsion, indexed by a multiplicatively cofinal subset of N≥1. The archimedean Kummer structure is reconstructed from compatible torsion-quotient systems, up to the unique nontrivial automorphism of the circle-like unit group.
Definition 4.9(vi)-(vii) Assemble the placewise constructions into F⊢ ×μ, and define the weakened F⊢×, F⊢×μ, and F⊢ ×μ-prime-strips by place-indexed isomorphism classes. This gives the coric, unit-sensitive prime-strip type preserved by the modified theta links.
Definition 4.9(viii) An F×μ-prime-strip is an F-prime-strip whose F⊢ portion is replaced by F⊢ ×μ. Bad-place generators and global comparison maps determine a pilot object of negative arithmetic degree. This is the object class on which Corollary 4.10 defines the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau full poly-isomorphisms.

×-Kummer structure

The ×-structure is a Z×-orbit of G-equivariant unit isomorphisms O×(G) -> O×(A). Mochizuki cites uniqueness from the preceding rigidity theory.

Def. 4.9(i)unique

×μ submodule invariant

A ×μ-structure is equivalently the family {I_H^κ(A)} inside O×μ(A) as H ranges over open subgroups of G.

Def. 4.9(i)torsion quotient

Bad-place construction

At Vbad, the monoid generated by μ_2l(A) and splitting images is quotiented by μ_2l(A). This supplies the value part of the direct product with units modulo torsion.

Def. 4.9(ii)-(iii)2l torsion

Good-place construction

At good nonarchimedean places, the splitting image determines O_perp(A); no additional 2l-torsion generator is inserted.

Def. 4.9(iv)splitting

Archimedean torsion system

At archimedean places, F⊢ ×μ is an inductive system of split Frobenioids obtained by quotienting units by N-torsion.

Def. 4.9(v)inductive

F⊢×μ prime-strip

Definition 4.9(vii) defines the weakened F⊢-prime-strip variants as placewise collections isomorphic to the data constructed in 4.9(vi).

Def. 4.9(vii)placewise

F×μ pilot object

In an F×μ-prime-strip, bad-place generators and the comparison maps determine a well-defined global realified Frobenioid object of negative arithmetic degree.

Def. 4.9(viii)pilot

Weakened link domain

The definition keeps enough unit/Kummer information for later log-wall comparison while deliberately weakening the full Frobenioid data that theta links do not preserve.

link domaincoric

Input to modified theta links

Corollary 4.10 immediately applies Definition 4.9 to constant, theta, and Gaussian F-prime-strips before defining the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau links.

Cor. 4.10next step
Source warning: F⊢×μ is not a decorative suffix on an F⊢-prime-strip. It records exactly the unit-quotient/Kummer interface whose coricity survives the modified theta links; the divisor/value-group component is treated separately.
Sources: IUT II, Definition 4.9 Related: ThetaNF Frobenioid localization Gaussian Frobenioids Theta×μ link Theta×μ-gau link Frobenioids/log-shells

IUT II Definition 4.9(i): Kummer Structure Orbits

Definition 4.9(i) starts from a Frobenioid over a base category equivalent to connected finite etale covers of a local or archimedean field. After choosing a universal covering pro-object A, the automorphism group G = Aut(A) acts on unit and torsion-quotient monoids. Kummer structures are not arbitrary extra labels: they are specified orbits of G-equivariant unit-isomorphisms reconstructed from G.

From universal cover to Kummer orbit Definition 4.9(i)
ItemSource definitionWiki consequence
Universal cover Choose a universal covering pro-object A of the base category and set G = Aut(A). The construction is invariant under the choice of A, because any two such pro-objects are isomorphic.
× structure A ×-Kummer structure is a Z×-orbit of G-equivariant isomorphisms from reconstructed units to O×(A). This unit interface is unique in the setting cited by Mochizuki, so it should not be modeled as a free choice downstream.
×μ structure A ×μ-Kummer structure is an Ism-orbit of isomorphisms to O×μ(A), equivalently the family of submodules I_H^kappa(A) for open H ⊆ G. The submodule family is the audit handle for the torsion-quotient Kummer data used in the modified theta links.
Sources: IUT II, Definition 4.9(i) Related: F×μ Kummer prime-strips placewise F⊢×μ construction Theta×μ links

IUT II Definition 4.9(ii)-(vi): Placewise F⊢×μ Construction

The weakened F⊢ ×μ data is assembled place by place. Bad nonarchimedean places include the 2l-torsion generator, good nonarchimedean places use the splitting image alone, and archimedean places use an inductive torsion-quotient system. The common output is a unit-sensitive, place-indexed Kummer interface.

Three local recipes, one weakened prime-strip layer Definition 4.9(ii)-(vi)
PlaceConstructionFormal role
Vbad Generate O_perp(A) from mu_2l(A) and splitting images, then quotient by mu_2l(A). Records the bad-place theta torsion used by the later pilot-object comparison.
Good nonarchimedean Use the image of the split Frobenioid splitting to define O_perp(A), then form the same O ×μ(A) direct product. Keeps the construction uniform without importing bad-place 2l-torsion.
Archimedean Replace the split Frobenioid by an inductive system over torsion quotients, modulo the nontrivial automorphism of the unit circle. Supplies the archimedean analogue of the Kummer quotient interface.
Sources: IUT II, Definition 4.9(ii)-(vi) Related: Kummer structure orbits F×μ pilot object Theta×μ links

IUT II Definition 4.9(vii)-(viii): F×μ Prime-Strips and Pilot Object

Definition 4.9(vii)-(viii) turns the placewise Kummer data into prime-strip types. An F×μ-prime-strip is an F-prime-strip whose mono-analytic F⊢ portion is replaced by F⊢ ×μ. At bad places, the generators of the value monoids and the comparison maps determine the associated pilot object of negative arithmetic degree.

Prime-strip upgrade to a pilot object Definition 4.9(vii)-(viii)
Formalization warning: the pilot object is not a free field of a prime-strip record. In the source definition it is determined, up to isomorphism, by bad-place generators together with the rho_v comparison data.
LayerDefinitionUse downstream
F⊢ variants For ⊢ ∈ {⊢×, ⊢×μ, ⊢ ×μ}, a prime-strip is a place-indexed collection isomorphic to the constructed weakened placewise data. Supplies the coric unit-sensitive interface preserved by modified theta links.
F×μ Replace the F⊢ component of an F-prime-strip by an F⊢ ×μ-prime-strip. Gives the domain/codomain class for Theta×μ links.
Pilot object Bad-place generators of O(A) and the rho_v maps determine an object of the global realified Frobenioid of negative arithmetic degree. Feeds the later theta-pilot comparison and log-volume corridor.
Sources: IUT II, Definition 4.9(vii)-(viii) Related: placewise F⊢×μ construction Theta×μ links Corollary 3.12 corridor

Theta×μ Frobenius-Pictures in IUT II 4.10

Corollary 4.10 applies the F×μ upgrade of Definition 4.9 to constant, theta, and Gaussian F-prime-strips. It defines the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau links, identifies their common coric F⊢×μ and D⊢ invariants, and organizes linked Θ±ellNF-Hodge theaters into oriented Frobenius-picture chains.

Corollary 4.10 link construction constant, theta, and Gaussian prime-strips through F×μ
Source itemConstructionMeaning
Corollary 4.10(i) The constant monoid data from the underlying Θ±ell-Hodge theater determines an F-prime-strip F_△, after identifying zero-label and diagonal-label constant data. This is the constant/model q-parameter target of the modified theta links and is naturally identified with Fmod.
Corollary 4.10(ii) Theta and Gaussian monoid data determine F_env and F_gau, with global realified Frobenioids and local comparison isomorphisms. Evaluation gives F_env -> F_gau. The Gaussian link is not independent of the theta link; it is obtained by composing with the evaluation isomorphism.
Corollary 4.10(iii) Applying Definition 4.9 functorially gives full poly-isomorphisms F×μ_env -> F×μ_△ and F×μ_gau -> F×μ_△. These are the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau links between Θ±ellNF-Hodge theaters.
Corollary 4.10(iv) Unit portions of theta and Gaussian monoids yield natural isomorphisms F⊢×μ_△ ≃ F⊢×μ_env ≃ F⊢×μ_gau. After composing with the link-induced poly-isomorphisms, the common F⊢×μ_△ and associated D⊢_△ are invariant. This is the coric part preserved by both modified theta links and the direct input for later log-wall comparison.
Corollary 4.10(v) The full poly-isomorphism of D⊢_△ data induces compatible isomorphisms of global realified Frobenioid collections, modulo R>0-orbits of arithmetic-degree scaling. The preserved D-side data also controls the realified global Frobenioid comparison, up to the expected scaling indeterminacy.
Corollary 4.10(vi) Iterating either modified theta link produces an infinite oriented graph of Θ±ellNF-Hodge theaters indexed by Z. The graph has translation symmetry but not arbitrary permutation symmetry; adjacent theater swaps are not automorphisms of this picture.
Remarks 4.10.1-4.10.3 Bad-place F-prime-strip indeterminacies are absorbed by full poly-isomorphisms. The Theta×μ_gau link is the later central link, but its construction as Theta×μ followed by evaluation is essential for multiradiality. Unit/coric data is preserved; divisor/value-group data is not. This isolates the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation: the volume distortion lives in the non-preserved value-group part.

Constant F_△

The constant prime-strip is built from Psi_cns and identified with Fmod. It supplies the constant/model side of both modified theta links.

Cor. 4.10(i)Fmod

Theta and Gaussian prime-strips

F_env comes from theta-environment monoids; F_gau comes from Gaussian monoids. The evaluation isomorphism relates them before the F×μ upgrade.

Cor. 4.10(ii)evaluation

Modified theta links

The Theta×μ link is F×μ_env -> F×μ_△. The Gaussian variant is F×μ_gau -> F×μ_△.

Cor. 4.10(iii)full poly-isomorphism

Coric F⊢×μ invariant

The common F⊢×μ_△ portion is invariant under both links. This is the unit/coric interface later measured through log-shells.

Cor. 4.10(iv)coric

D-Theta±ellNF link

The preserved F⊢×μ_△ data induces the full poly-isomorphism of associated D⊢_△ prime-strips, called the D-Theta±ellNF-link.

Cor. 4.10(iv)D-side

Coric realified Frobenioids

Corollary 4.10(v) transports global realified Frobenioid collections along the preserved D⊢_△ data, modulo R>0-orbits of arithmetic-degree scaling.

Cor. 4.10(v)realified

Frobenius-picture chain

Iterating the modified theta links gives an oriented integer-indexed chain of Hodge theaters. Translation by Z is allowed; arbitrary permutation of vertices is not.

Cor. 4.10(vi)oriented

Why keep Theta×μ before Gaussian evaluation

Remark 4.10.2 says the later focus is the Gaussian link, but constructing it as the theta link followed by Galois evaluation is crucial for the multiradiality used in IUT III Theorem 3.11.

Rem. 4.10.2multiradial

Value-group distortion

Remark 4.10.3 says divisor/value-group data is not preserved by the links. This non-preserved part is the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation whose volume effect is computed later.

Rem. 4.10.3not preserved
Source warning: Corollary 4.10 does not say that the full Frobenioid is preserved across theta links. The preserved common data is the coric F⊢×μ/D⊢ interface; the value-group distortion is intentionally left as the arithmetic deformation.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.10 and Remarks 4.10.1-4.10.3 Related: F×μ Kummer prime-strips ThetaNF Frobenioid localization Gaussian Frobenioids Frobenioids/log-shells theta/log links

IUT II Corollary 4.10(iii)-(v): Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau Links

Corollary 4.10 applies Definition 4.9 functorially to constant, theta, and Gaussian prime-strips. The resulting full poly-isomorphisms are the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau links. Their shared coric F⊢×μ portion and the associated D⊢ prime-strip are invariant through the link.

Modified theta links preserve the coric unit layer Corollary 4.10(iii)-(v)
ClauseConstructionConsequence
4.10(iii) Functoriality of Definition 4.9 sends F-prime-strip isomorphisms to F×μ-prime-strip poly-isomorphisms. Defines the two modified theta links between Θ±ellNF-Hodge theaters.
4.10(iv) The unit portions give natural isomorphisms between F⊢×μ_△, F⊢×μ_env, and F⊢×μ_gau. The common F⊢×μ_△ portion is coric for both modified links and induces the D⊢_△ link.
4.10(v) The D⊢_△ poly-isomorphism induces compatible isomorphisms of global realified Frobenioids, modulo R>0-degree scaling. The realified global comparison is controlled by the coric D-side invariant, not by a raw identification of source histories.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.10(iii)-(v) Related: F×μ pilot object Frobenius-picture chain D-Theta etale-pictures

IUT II Corollary 4.10(vi): Oriented Frobenius-Picture Chain

Iterating the modified theta links gives an infinite chain of Θ±ellNF-Hodge theaters indexed by the integers. Mochizuki emphasizes that the chain has a translation symmetry but not arbitrary permutation symmetry: the order of adjacent theaters is part of the picture.

Ordered chain, not an unordered family Corollary 4.10(vi), Remarks 4.10.2-4.10.3
FeatureSource contentWhy it matters
Infinite chain A collection {HT_n}_{n in Z} of distinct Θ±ellNF-Hodge theaters is linked by repeated Theta×μ or Theta×μ_gau arrows. The chain is the modified Frobenius-picture analogue used before the etale-picture passage.
Order consciousness The picture admits integer translation symmetry, but it is not symmetric under arbitrary transpositions of adjacent theaters. This prevents treating the modified theta-link direction as a mere undirected equivalence relation.
Next transition Corollary 4.11 passes from these Frobenius-pictures to D-Θ±ellNF etale-pictures. The wiki keeps the ordered Frobenius chain separate from the later etale-picture full poly-isomorphism class.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.10(vi) and Remarks 4.10.2-4.10.3 Related: Theta×μ links D-Theta etale-pictures log-theta lattice

D-Theta Etale-Pictures in IUT II 4.11

Corollary 4.11 converts the oriented Frobenius-picture chains of Corollary 4.10 into D-side etale-pictures. The preserved D⊢_△ prime-strip becomes a common mono-analytic core, and the surrounding D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters become spokes that admit arbitrary permutation symmetries. This is the clean contrast with the ordered Theta×μ chain.

From Frobenius-picture to etale-picture same D⊢ core, permutable D-Hodge-theater spokes
Source itemConstructionMeaning
Corollary 4.11(i) The D-Theta±ellNF-linked chain induced by Corollary 4.10 gives a chain of full poly-isomorphisms ... -> D⊢_△ -> D⊢_△ -> .... Thus D⊢_△ is constant through the chain. This is the mono-analytic core shared by the D-side theaters.
Corollary 4.11(ii) Each D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater is regarded as a spoke emanating from the common D⊢_△ core. Unlike the oriented Frobenius-picture chain, this etale-picture admits arbitrary permutation symmetries among the spokes.
Corollary 4.11(iii) The construction is compatible with the earlier IUT I etale-pictures after identifying the relevant D⊢ data. The enhanced IUT II picture refines, rather than replaces, the base Hodge-theater etale-picture.
Remark 4.11.1 The Frobenius-picture is order-conscious iteration of Gaussian theta-exponent transformations, whereas the etale-picture corresponds to commutativity of the same exponent data under arbitrary permutations. Mochizuki compares this to cartesian versus polar-coordinate representations of the Gaussian integral.
Remark 4.11.2 The review explains how Gaussian distributions bridge theta functions and number fields, why conjugate synchronization is needed for units, why the post-anabelian Gaussian side constructs the link, and why the anabelian side is needed for the later log-wall. This is the local source of the wiki distinction between F_l^± unit/conjugate-synchronized behavior and F_l value-group/multiradial behavior.

D⊢ mono-analytic core

Corollary 4.11(i) makes D⊢_△ the common invariant across the D-Theta±ellNF-linked chain. It is not a full holomorphic structure; it is the D-side mono-analytic core.

Cor. 4.11(i)core

Permutable D-theater spokes

Corollary 4.11(ii) treats the D-Hodge theaters as spokes around the common core. Arbitrary permutation symmetries are available at this D-side etale-picture level.

Cor. 4.11(ii)spokes

Frobenius versus etale order

The Frobenius-picture remembers an ordered Z-chain; the etale-picture forgets this order enough to permit spoke permutations around the common core.

Cor. 4.10-4.11symmetry

Theta-exponent commutativity

Remark 4.11.1 reads the etale-picture as commutativity among the theta-exponent factors that appear in iterated Gaussian q -> q^{j^2}-type transformations.

Rem. 4.11.1Gaussian analogy

Gaussian bridge review

Remark 4.11.2 describes the Gaussian distribution as the bridge between theta functions at bad primes and number fields, and as the source of the non-scheme-theoretic Theta×μ_gau link.

Rem. 4.11.2(i)bridge

Conjugate synchronization for units

The connected Kummer-compatible theta-evaluation theater establishes conjugate synchronization compatible with F_l^±-symmetry, enabling coricity of units.

Rem. 4.11.2(ii)units

Post-anabelian and anabelian Gaussian sides

The post-anabelian Gaussian representation constructs the Theta×μ_gau link; the anabelian representation is retained for the IUT III log-wall.

Rem. 4.11.2(ii)two representations

F_l versus F_l± roles

Remark 4.11.2 contrasts F_l^± as unit-side/uniradial and compatible with conjugate synchronization with F_l as value-group/multiradial and tied to global NF-bridge bookkeeping.

Rem. 4.11.2(iii)two symmetries

Prime-tree dismantling

The review says the number-field obstruction is the prime-tree structure that blocks conjugate synchronization; labelled prime-strips are finite approximations used to dismantle this obstruction.

Rem. 4.11.2(iv)prime-strips
Source warning: etale-picture permutation symmetry is D-side and mono-analytic. It should not be imported back into the ordered Frobenius-picture chain of Theta×μ or Theta×μ_gau links.
Sources: IUT II, Corollary 4.11 and Remarks 4.11.1-4.11.2 Related: Theta×μ Frobenius-pictures IUT I theta/Frobenius pictures ThetaNF Fl symmetry multiradiality log-theta lattice

IUT II Remark 4.11.3: Line-Bundle Log-Wall Juggling

Remark 4.11.3 compares two alignments that run in opposite directions. Combinatorially, the Thetaell-bridge belongs to the additive F_l^± symmetry and the NF-bridge to the multiplicative F_l symmetry. At the line-bundle level, however, arithmetic line bundles are treated multiplicatively in the Thetaell-bridge, while their module-line-bundle interpretation requires the global ring structure of the NF-bridge.

Two crossed correspondences Remark 4.11.3
LevelRemark 4.11.3 correspondenceFormal reading
Symmetry combinatorics Thetaell aligns with additive F_l^±; NF aligns with multiplicative F_l. Keep the two label symmetries distinct; they encode different dimensions.
Line bundles Arithmetic line bundles are treated multiplicatively by monoids and Frobenioids in the Thetaell bridge, while equivalence with module-line-bundle data uses the global ring structure of number fields on the NF side. Do not collapse line-bundle meanings into one untyped object.
Log-wall Mochizuki identifies this crossing as a combinatorial counterpart of the multiplicative/additive juggling in the log-wall. This page is a bridge from IUT II Gaussian data to IUT III log-shells.
Sources: IUT II, Remark 4.11.3 Related: ThetaNF Fl symmetry synchronization/log-wall Frobenioids/log-shells

IUT II Remark 4.11.4: Hodge-Arakelov and p-Adic Teichmuller Analogy

Remark 4.11.4 explains how the IUT II Gaussian construction should be read against Hodge-Arakelov theory and p-adic Teichmuller theory. The l-torsion labels are rough finite approximations to deformation dimensions, the Gaussian values behave like a deformation between identity and Frobenius, and the paired F_l^±/F_l symmetries echo additive and multiplicative monodromy patterns in canonical lifting.

Three analogy layers Remark 4.11.4 and correction Remark 4.11.5
Remark itemAnalogyWiki consequence
4.11.4(i) l-torsion points are rough finite approximations to the two real deformation dimensions; theta values at labels represent possible deformations of holomorphic structure. Gaussian value profiles are deformation data, not bare numerical labels.
4.11.4(ii) The semi-infinite Theta×μ chain resembles canonical lifting, while passage to the Gaussian chain resembles the move from MF∇-objects to Galois representations. This explains why the Gaussian link is kept as a representation-facing object.
4.11.4(iii)(c) F_l^± is additive/uniradial and analogous to ordinary-locus unipotent monodromy; F_l is multiplicative/multiradial and analogous to supersingular toral monodromy. The additive/multiplicative symmetry split is a structural input.
4.11.4(iii)(d), 4.11.5 Conjugate synchronization is compared with deformation links in cohomology. Remark 4.11.5 then corrects Tate-twist signs and a cohomology-submodule dimension condition in the cited canonical-lifting statement. The analogy is source guidance, but formal references to the canonical lifting lemma must use the corrected notation.
Sources: IUT II, Remarks 4.11.4-4.11.5 Related: Frobenius-picture chain ThetaNF Fl symmetry theta evaluation route

Theta Evaluation Route

IUT II begins by relating Frobenioid-theoretic theta functions to etale/Galois-theoretic theta functions through Kummer theory. The goal is to evaluate the reciprocal theta function at torsion labels in a form that can later be treated as Gaussian Frobenioid data.

Kummer square for theta evaluation Frobenius-like monoids are compared to etale-like Galois data
IUT II source item Construction Why it is separate from Gaussian Frobenioids
Introduction, Fig. I.1 Kummer theory relates the Frobenioid-theoretic theta function to the etale theta function, and after evaluation relates Frobenioid-theoretic theta values to Galois-theoretic theta values. Gaussian Frobenioids use theta values as input; this page records the preceding evaluation and comparison route.
Introduction At a bad place v, theta values at torsion labels are, up to a 2l-th root of unity, of the form q_v^(j^2) for j in |F_l|. The square weight appears before the global log-volume comparison. It is not an artifact of later bookkeeping.
Definition 1.1, Propositions 1.2-1.5 Mono-theta environments package the tempered group, theta-section data, interior/exterior cyclotomes, and reconstruction routes from either topological groups or tempered Frobenioids. They supply the rigidity interface used to pass between group-theoretic and Frobenioid-theoretic theta data.
Corollaries 1.10 and 1.12 Cyclotomic rigidity and constant-multiple rigidity are formulated multiradially, with zero-label splittings controlling scalar ambiguity. These rigidity inputs are needed before theta values can become stable value-profile data.
Remark 1.12.4 Galois evaluation is forced by functoriality with respect to coverings and Kummer classes: the evaluation algorithm is represented by a section from the local Galois group into the relevant covering group. Evaluation points are not arbitrary analytic choices; they are recovered in a group-theoretic form compatible with Kummer theory.
Corollaries 2.4-2.8 Decomposition groups attached to two-torsion-shifted cusp labels produce theta-value orbits; mono-theta cyclotomic rigidity then replaces l DeltaTheta by the exterior cyclotome. This is the concrete path from labels to Kummer-compatible theta values.

Theta function to values

IUT II evaluates the reciprocal l-th root of the theta function at torsion labels, shifted by a suitable two-torsion point. The resulting values are controlled by the q_v^(j^2) profile.

IUT II Introj^2

Kummer bridge

Kummer theory is the bridge between Frobenius-like monoids and etale-like Galois data. This bridge is applied both before and after theta evaluation.

Fig. I.1Frobenius/etale

Mono-theta rigidities

Discrete rigidity limits unwanted profinite powers; cyclotomic rigidity identifies interior and exterior cyclotomes; constant-multiple rigidity gives the zero-label splitting of theta monoids.

IUT II §1rigidity

Galois evaluation principle

Remark 1.12.4 explains that Kummer-compatible evaluation must extend to coverings and therefore be functorial for Galois groups. This makes the evaluation algorithm group-theoretic.

Rem. 1.12.4section

Single basepoint

Remark 2.6.1 states that all theta values for |t| in |F_l| are computed relative to one local Galois group and one cyclotome. This is conjugate synchronization.

Rem. 2.6.1synchronized

Distinguished subgraph

Remark 2.6.2 identifies the subgraph producing the q_v^(j^2) collection as distinguished by a maximality condition compatible with the global product formula.

Rem. 2.6.2product formula
Formalization warning: theta evaluation should not be modeled as a direct map from labels to numbers. The source route passes through mono-theta rigidity, decomposition groups, Kummer classes, and a single synchronized cyclotome.
Sources: IUT II, Introduction and §§1-2 Related: Galois theta evaluation Theta±ell relay Gaussian Frobenioids multiradiality theta/log links

Gaussian Monoids and Frobenioids

IUT II turns theta values into Gaussian monoids and then into local and global Frobenioid data. This is the source of the j^2 weighting and of the Theta×μ/Theta×μ-gau links that feed the later log-theta-lattice and log-volume arguments.

IUT II Gaussian pipeline from theta evaluation to coric prime-strips
StageSource objectConstruction roleFormalization note
Theta monoids Psi_env, Psi_cns, and Frobenioid-theoretic theta monoids. Connect mono-theta environments, Kummer classes, and Frobenioid theta functions. Must remember whether the construction is group-theoretic, Frobenioid-theoretic, or already post-anabelian.
Gaussian monoids Value-profile-indexed monoids Psi_xi and Psi_Fxi. Package theta values by labeled value-profiles and their splittings. Labels and j^2-weighting are not optional metadata.
Frobenioid interpretation F_cns, F_env, F_gau, and related split Frobenioids. Definition 3.8 turns monoids with Galois action into local Frobenioids of monoid type Z. Separates divisor-monoid structure, splittings, and the underlying topological group action.
Post-anabelian forgetting Abstract Gaussian monoids stripped of the construction algorithm. Remark 3.6.2 describes the final "forget" operation after Kummer/Galois evaluation. Useful only after the construction path has been recorded; otherwise it erases source obligations.
IUT II §3 source item Local monoid construction Why it is proof-critical
Proposition 3.1(i) A projective system of mono-theta environments produces split theta monoids Psi_env and infty Psi_env, generated from the constant part and theta subsets inside cohomology limits. These monoids retain the theta data together with splittings up to torsion, so they are the multiradial-friendly input for Gaussian construction.
Proposition 3.1(ii) The same environment also produces a constant monoid Psi_cns, naturally isomorphic to the local base-field monoid and equipped with a local Galois action. Constant monoids are necessary for value-profiles, but their construction uses the base-field cyclotome route that later causes uniradiality.
Example 3.2, Proposition 3.3 When the mono-theta environment comes from a tempered Frobenioid, Kummer classes identify Frobenioid-theoretic theta monoids and constant monoids with the mono-theta-theoretic versions. This ties Hodge-Arakelov theta evaluation back to the Frobenioid objects that live inside Hodge theaters.
Proposition 3.4 Split theta monoids are compatible with the O×mu-version of the theta link, hence multiradial; constant monoids, as constructed, are only uniradial. This is an explicit source-level warning: not every useful local monoid is automatically stable under the multiradial comparison demanded later.
Corollary 3.5(i) The F_l^± symmetry gives symmetrizing isomorphisms between labeled copies of local Galois/constant-monoid data without crushing the label set. Gaussian monoids require distinct values at distinct labels while still using one synchronized basepoint/cyclotome.
Corollary 3.5(ii)-(iii) A value-profile is a label-indexed tuple of theta-value elements. Gaussian monoids are generated by the diagonal unit part and powers or roots of the value-profile; zero-label restriction gives splittings up to torsion. This is the local source of the Gaussian distribution that later becomes a log-volume object.
Remark 3.5.2 Symmetrizing isomorphisms must preserve a bijective relationship with the label set and the single basepoint. Naively quotienting labels to one point fails this requirement. This reinforces the wiki rule that label erasure and label synchronization are different operations.
IUT II's Figure 3.1 can be read as a four-step diagnostic: Frobenioid-theoretic theta monoids pass through Kummer theory to group-theoretic theta monoids, Galois evaluation produces group-theoretic Gaussian monoids, and a final forgetful step yields post-anabelian Gaussian monoids. The wiki keeps these stages explicit because later Lean interfaces must not silently replace one stage by another.
IUT II §4 source item Object or link Formal meaning for the wiki
Definition 4.9(i) ×- and ×μ-Kummer structures on a Frobenioid, expressed as orbits of isomorphisms O×(G) -> O×(A) and O×μ(G) -> O×μ(A). This fixes the unit-quotient interface used later by theta links. ×-Kummer structures are unique; a ×μ-Kummer structure is equivalently the system of invariant submodules I_H^κ(A).
Definition 4.9(ii)-(vi) From an F⊢-prime-strip, construct local F⊢×, F⊢×μ, and F⊢ ×μ data at bad, good nonarchimedean, and archimedean places. The construction is place-sensitive: bad places use the 2l-torsion quotient generated by splittings, good nonarchimedean places use the splitting image, and archimedean places use an inductive system of torsion quotients.
Definition 4.9(vii)-(viii) F⊢×μ-prime-strips and F×μ-prime-strips, including the pilot object of negative arithmetic degree determined by the bad-place generators. This is the precise object class on which the modified theta links act. It is stronger than a bare coric category but weaker than preservation of the full original Frobenioid.
Corollary 4.10(i) Constant prime-strips F_△, naturally identified with Fmod. The target side of the link is the constant/model q-parameter prime-strip, not a new unrelated global object.
Corollary 4.10(ii) Theta and Gaussian prime-strips F_env and F_gau, naturally identified with Ftht and related by evaluation. The Gaussian side is produced from the theta side by the evaluation isomorphism before the F×μ upgrade.
Corollary 4.10(iii) Full poly-isomorphisms F×μ_env -> F×μ_△ and F×μ_gau -> F×μ_△. These are the Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau links. Their definition depends on functorial passage from F-prime-strip isomorphisms to F×μ-prime-strip isomorphisms.
Corollary 4.10(iv)-(v) Coric invariants F⊢×μ_△ and D⊢_△, plus associated global realified Frobenioids modulo R>0-orbits. The preserved part is the unit/coric interface. It is exactly the portion later used when log-wall and volume comparisons require common mono-analytic data.
Corollary 4.10(vi), Remark 4.10.3 Infinite oriented chains of linked theaters and non-preserved divisor/value-group data. Translation by Z is allowed, arbitrary permutation of theaters is not. The value-group distortion is the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation whose log-volume effect is computed later.
IUT II Corollary 4.11 source item Etale-picture construction Why it matters later
Corollary 4.11(i) The chain of D-Theta±ellNF-linked D-Hodge theaters induces a chain of full poly-isomorphisms ... -> nD⊢_△ -> (n+1)D⊢_△ -> .... Thus (-)D⊢_△ is a constant invariant. This supplies the mono-analytic core shared by the otherwise distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures.
Corollary 4.11(ii) Each D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater is a spoke emanating from the common mono-analytic core. The spokes admit arbitrary permutation symmetries. This contrasts with the oriented Frobenius-picture chain, which has translation symmetry but not arbitrary adjacent swaps.
Corollary 4.11(iii) The construction is compatible with the earlier IUT I etale-pictures after identifying D⊢_△ with the appropriate underlying D⊢_> data. The IUT II enhanced picture is not a separate formal universe; it refines the IUT I base-theater picture.
Remark 4.11.1 The Frobenius-picture is order-conscious iteration of Gaussian theta-exponent transformations, while the etale-picture treats the corresponding exponents with permutation-symmetric commutativity. This is the local source of the later distinction between ordered theta-link motion and common mono-analytic comparison data.
Remark 4.11.2 Mochizuki reviews the role of Gaussian distributions, conjugate synchronization, post-anabelian and anabelian Gaussian representations, and unit/value-group splittings. The review explains why the link construction needs both the post-anabelian Gaussian side and the anabelian/log-wall side developed in IUT III.
Frobenius-picture versus etale-picture IUT II Corollary 4.10 to Corollary 4.11

Theta monoids before Gaussian monoids

IUT II Proposition 3.1 constructs theta monoids and constant monoids from projective systems of mono-theta environments. Propositions 3.3 and 3.4 compare the mono-theta, Frobenioid-theoretic, and group-theoretic versions using Kummer classes and rigidity isomorphisms.

This is the local algebraic input for the later Gaussian construction: theta data is first made into monoids that can be acted on, compared, and stripped to unit/value-group components.

IUT II, Proposition 3.1Proposition 3.4

Constant monoids

The constant monoid Psi_cns is reconstructed from the base-field part by using the natural valuation surjection. It is essential for Gaussian value-profiles but is the first local monoid in this chain whose source construction is only uniradial as stated.

IUT III's log-shell passage is therefore not decorative: it is needed to repair this route into a multiradial form with controlled indeterminacy.

Proposition 3.1(ii)uniradial

Symmetrizing isomorphisms

Corollary 3.5 uses the F_l^± symmetry to compare labeled local Galois/constant-monoid data without identifying all labels with one coarse quotient point.

Remark 3.5.2 isolates the rule: keep the bijective relationship with the label set while using one synchronized basepoint.

Corollary 3.5(i)labels

Gaussian monoids and value-profiles

Corollary 3.5 defines value-profiles and Gaussian monoids from symmetrized labeled constant monoids. The construction depends on F_l^±-symmetrizing isomorphisms and on preserving a bijective relationship with the label set; Remark 3.5.2 contrasts this with crushing labels to a single quotient point.

Corollary 3.6 gives the corresponding Frobenioid-theoretic Gaussian monoids when the mono-theta environment comes from a tempered Frobenioid.

Corollary 3.5Corollary 3.6value-profile

Globalization over all places

IUT II §4 extends the Gaussian construction from bad places to good nonarchimedean and archimedean places. Corollary 4.5 then attaches constant monoids, semi-simplifications, local theta/Gaussian monoids, and global realified theta/Gaussian Frobenioids to a D-Theta±ell-Hodge theater.

The global Frobenioids D_env and D_gau carry realified divisor data, so this layer is already preparing the objects whose log-volumes are estimated later.

IUT II §4Corollary 4.5

Kummer Frobenioid structures

Definition 4.9 starts from a Frobenioid whose base category is the category of connected finite etale covers of a local or archimedean field. A universal covering pro-object A has automorphism group G; Kummer structures compare group-theoretically reconstructed unit quotients with the units evaluated at A.

The ×-structure is unique. The ×μ-structure keeps torsion quotients and is recorded by the family I_H^κ(A) as H ranges over open subgroups of G.

Definition 4.9(i)Kummer

F×μ-prime-strips

Definition 4.9 upgrades an F⊢-prime-strip to the F⊢×μ and F×μ interfaces by keeping unit quotients and controlled splitting data while weakening the original Frobenioid.

At bad places the monoid generated by 2l-torsion and splitting images supplies the quotient used in the unit/value monoid O×μ(A). The resulting F×μ-prime-strip also carries a pilot object of negative arithmetic degree.

Definition 4.9(ii)-(viii)link domain

Constant prime-strips

Corollary 4.10(i) turns the constant monoid data Psi_cns(F≻)_t into F-prime-strips and then identifies the zero-label and diagonal-label versions. The resulting F_△ is naturally identified with Fmod.

This is the q-parameter or model side of the modified theta links.

Corollary 4.10(i)Fmod

Theta and Gaussian prime-strips

Corollary 4.10(ii) constructs F_env from theta-environment monoids and F_gau from Gaussian monoids, together with global realified Frobenioids and local comparison isomorphisms.

The evaluation isomorphism F_env -> F_gau is the source of the Gaussian version of the link, not an extra symmetry imposed after the fact.

Corollary 4.10(ii)evaluation

Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau links

Corollary 4.10 constructs F-prime-strips from constant, theta, and Gaussian data, passes them to F⊢×μ-prime-strips, and defines the Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau links between Theta±ellNF theaters.

The coric output is the invariant F⊢×μ_△ and the associated D⊢_△ prime-strip. The divisor or value-group part is not preserved by these links; Remark 4.10.3 identifies that distortion as the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation whose volume effect is later computed.

Corollary 4.10Remark 4.10.3

Value-group distortion

Remark 4.10.3 separates preserved unit/coric data from the divisor or value-group part, which is explicitly not preserved by the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau links.

Mochizuki describes this non-preserved part as the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation. Restricting the Gaussian value-group correspondence to the label j = 1 recovers the pivotal distribution, but restriction to proper label subsets generally breaks the relevant F_l^{±} and F_l symmetries.

Remark 4.10.3not preserved

D-theater mono-analytic core

Corollary 4.11(i) states that the D-prime-strip D⊢_△ is constant across the D-Theta±ellNF linked chain. This is the shared mono-analytic core of the etale-picture.

The point is not that all theaters have one holomorphic history, but that their D-theater shadows meet in a common mono-analytic invariant.

Corollary 4.11(i)mono-analytic

Etale-picture

Corollary 4.11(ii) draws D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters as spokes around the common D⊢_△ core. Unlike the oriented Frobenius-picture, this spoke picture admits arbitrary permutation symmetries among the labels.

This distinction is a recurring audit rule: ordered theta motion and common mono-analytic comparison data are different structures.

Corollary 4.11(ii)spokes

Arithmetic holomorphic spokes

Each spoke in the etale-picture is a distinct arithmetic holomorphic structure on the same mono-analytic core. Corollary 4.11 makes this explicit for the D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters obtained from the Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau chains.

The same construction remains compatible with the earlier IUT I base etale-pictures after passing to the relevant underlying D-theaters.

Corollary 4.11(ii)-(iii)holomorphic structure

Theta-exponent commutativity

Remark 4.11.1 explains the contrast in terms of Gaussian theta exponents: the Frobenius-picture remembers an order of repeated q -> q^{j^2}-type transformations, while the etale-picture packages the associated exponents with permutation symmetry.

Mochizuki compares this to the cartesian versus polar-coordinate representations of the Gaussian integral.

Remark 4.11.1Gaussian analogy
Link to Corollary 3.12: the Gaussian layer explains where the square-weighted theta-value distribution enters the later log-volume inequality. It also clarifies why the wiki keeps unit/coric information distinct from divisor or value-group information.
Sources: IUT II, §§3-4 Related: multiplicative/additive bridges theta/log links log-theta lattice Corollary 3.12 corridor

Frobenioids and Log-Shells

Frobenioids and log-shells are two complementary interfaces in IUT. Frobenioids package divisor, line-bundle, and monoid data categorically; log-shells provide the mono-analytic logarithmic receptacles in which the later multiradial algorithms are expressed.

Multiplicative package to logarithmic receptacle bad-prime Frobenioids, Gaussian monoids, and log-shell versions
IUT I §5 source item Construction Role in the later theory
Remark 5.2.1 F-prime-strips functorially determine D-prime-strips; F⊢-prime-strips determine D⊢-prime-strips. An F-prime-strip also has a mono-analyticization to an F⊢-prime-strip. This justifies treating Frobenioid data as lying over the D-strip layer rather than as an unrelated package.
Remark 5.2.2 F-prime-strips are Kummer-ready, while F⊢-prime-strips are Kummer-blind. The wiki keeps Kummer-ready and mono-analytic data separate because later Kummer transport cannot be recovered from the weakened interface alone.
Definition 5.2(v)-(vi) At nonarchimedean places, the D-side fundamental group reconstructs rational-function pseudo-monoids M^∞κ and M^∞κ×. Kummer classes determine cyclotomic identifications and the uniquely determined coric structures attached to an F-prime-strip. This is one source of the later distinction between Kummer-ready Frobenioid data and data visible only after mono-analytic weakening.
Definition 5.2(vii)-(viii) At archimedean places, Aut-holomorphic reconstruction supplies meromorphic-function pseudo-monoids and Kummer-structure cyclotomic isomorphisms; the ∞κ structure is recovered from ∞κ× by testing torsion at strictly critical points. The archimedean side has its own reconstruction mechanism. Treating all places as if they were nonarchimedean would lose a source distinction in the paper.
Corollary 5.3 Isomorphisms of F-prime-strips are determined by the associated D-prime-strip isomorphisms; for F⊢-prime-strips the corresponding map to D⊢-isomorphisms is surjective. This is a rigidity statement: once the base side is fixed, much of the Frobenioid-side isomorphism data is forced.
Example 5.4(iii)-(iv) A cusp-label class delta in LabCusp(D) determines δ-valuations in V(D); localizing at them constructs an F-prime-strip F|delta, well-defined up to isomorphism. This is where global cusp-label synchronization becomes a Frobenioid localization mechanism.
Example 5.4(v)-(vii) There is a unique NF-side poly-morphism psi^NF : F_J -> F lying over phi^NF. Restriction functors attached to Fmod are immune to the relevant F_l-conjugacy choices; the case j = 1 gives the pivotal distribution. Pivotal distributions are the number-field/Frobenioid precursor to later theta-value and q-parameter comparisons.

Categorical package

IUT I describes a Frobenioid as a categorical abstraction of line bundles or divisor monoids over a base category of topological localizations. This is the source of the F-prime-strip interfaces, where the concrete bad-prime models include O_Fv^▹, O_Fv^× × q_v^N, and their quotient variants.

Bad-prime theta package

In IUT I, Example 3.2, a bad nonarchimedean place produces a tempered Frobenioid F_v, its birationalization, a Frobenioid-theoretic reciprocal theta value Theta_v, and split Frobenioids F_v^| and F_v^Theta. The constructions are asserted to be reconstructible from categorical data.

Delta-valuations

In IUT I, Example 5.4, a class delta in LabCusp(D) selects valuations of D by transporting delta to the distinguished cusp class and then reading off the corresponding image of the D_j component labeled 1.

This definition can also be read intrinsically from the D-NF bridge. The point is that the label-class synchronization of Proposition 4.7 becomes a localization rule for Frobenioid data.

Example 5.4(iii)localization

F-prime-strip F|delta

Localizing at the δ-valuations constructs an F-prime-strip F|delta. At nonarchimedean places this uses p-adic Frobenioids and suitable open subgroups; at bad places the tempered analogue is required; at archimedean places the construction uses Aut-holomorphic reconstruction.

Because the projection from the relevant valuation set to Vmod is not injective, the result is only well-defined up to isomorphism.

Example 5.4(iv)F-prime-strip

Kappa-coric structures

Definition 5.2 attaches ∞κ and ∞κ× pseudo-monoid structures to F-prime-strips. Nonarchimedean components use group-theoretic reconstruction and Kummer cohomology; archimedean components use Aut-holomorphic meromorphic functions.

The ∞κ structure is extracted from ∞κ× by imposing torsion/root-of-unity behavior at strictly critical points.

Definition 5.2(vi),(viii)Kummer

Pivotal distributions

Example 5.4 defines natural restriction functors and realifications for the Frobenioid data over the capsule J. The objects obtained in the label j = 1 case are called pivotal distributions.

These distributions are immune to the relevant F_l-conjugacy indeterminacy because the Fmod object is defined through terminal objects of the global D-side category.

Example 5.4(vii)pivotal

Gaussian Frobenioids

IUT II turns Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic theta evaluations into Gaussian monoids and then interprets those monoids as local Frobenioids. This is where the Frobenioid-theoretic and mono-theta-theoretic versions are compared before the theta-link machinery is assembled.

Log-shell versions

For a topological group G isomorphic to a local Galois group, IUT II recalls the log-shell I(G) subset O×μ(G). Replacing raw O×μ(G) data by the pair (O×μ(G), I(G)) yields log-shell versions of multiradial environments.

LayerSource roleWhy it matters later
D and D⊢ bases Categories of coverings or Galois-category data attached to local curves. Separate holomorphic local structure from mono-analytic structure.
Frobenioids Monoid/divisor/line-bundle package over the base category. Carry theta and q-parameter information in a categorical form.
Gaussian monoids Monoids obtained from theta evaluations at torsion labels. Supply the weighted multiplicative input for later log-volume estimates.
Log-shells Subobjects inside local unit-quotient data, controlled by local logarithms. Make the relevant algorithms multiradial, at the cost of mild indeterminacy.
The formalization burden is not merely to name these objects. A useful Lean model must represent which information is intrinsic to the category, which information is transported through Frobenioid isomorphisms, and which information is visible only after passage to the mono-analytic log-shell layer.
Sources: IUT I IUT II IUT III Related: log-theta lattice Gaussian Frobenioids multiradiality

Theta Links and Log Links

The two principal directions of the log-theta lattice are different in kind. Theta links come from Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic evaluation of theta values; log links come from applying local logarithms and passing through log-shells.

Two link directions horizontal theta motion versus vertical logarithmic motion
LinkSourceFunction in the theoryRisk if misread
Theta link IUT I Full poly-isomorphism from the theta-side F-prime-strip to the model q-parameter F-prime-strip. Reading it as an ordinary ring/scheme morphism remembers too much.
Theta×μ link IUT II Modified theta link that preserves the weakened F⊢×μ prime-strip portion. Treating it as preservation of the whole Frobenioid hides the value-group loss.
Theta×μ_gau link IUT II Gaussian version obtained after replacing theta monoids by Gaussian monoids. Forgetting the Gaussian replacement loses the source of later j^2-weighted data.
Theta×μ_LGP / lgp links IUT III LGP/log-shell versions used in the log-theta lattice and multiradial representation. Collapsing them into the earlier theta link erases the log-shell stage.
Log link IUT III Applies local p-adic logarithmic structures and induces log-shell comparisons along a vertical line. Forgetting calibration and upper-semi behavior can destroy the intended comparison meaning.

Theta and Gaussian prime-strips

IUT II Corollary 4.10 constructs environment and Gaussian F-prime-strips from theta/Gaussian monoids, then compares their associated F⊢×μ-prime-strips by full poly-isomorphisms.

Coric unit portion

The F⊢×μ and associated D⊢ material is coric across the theta and Gaussian links. This is the source for later claims that selected weakened data can be treated independently of a particular holomorphic history.

Vertical obligation

IUT III Remark 1.4.1 says that executing a horizontal theta arrow obligates subsequent vertical log motion if one wants the log-shell tools used in the multiradial representation.

Noncommutativity

Horizontal links are incompatible with local ring structures in a different way from vertical log links. The lattice square is therefore not a commuting square of ordinary schemes or rings.

Translation symmetry

The Frobenius-picture chains admit integer translation symmetry, but not arbitrary permutation symmetry. Adjacent alien copies cannot simply be swapped while leaving the rest fixed.

Formalization pressure

A Lean interface must say whether a map is a theta link, Gaussian theta link, log link, or induced coric prime-strip comparison. Reusing one generic arrow type would hide essential source distinctions.

A useful mnemonic is that theta-type links are horizontal filters between mutually alien Hodge theaters, while log-links are vertical logarithmic operations inside a fixed arithmetic holomorphic structure. The log-theta lattice is built precisely because both operations are needed and they do not commute as ordinary maps.
Sources: IUT II IUT III Related: log-theta lattice Frobenioids/log-shells multiradiality

Log-Theta Lattice

The log-theta lattice is the two-dimensional noncommutative diagram of Hodge theaters studied in IUT III. Horizontal motion is theta-link motion; vertical motion is log-link motion. The noncommutativity is not incidental: it is a source of the comparison constraints, coricity distinctions, and indeterminacies.

Log-theta lattice horizontal theta links, vertical log links
IUT III introduction source item Construction Constraint recorded in the wiki
Abstract and Introduction The log-theta lattice is a noncommutative two-dimensional diagram of Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters. Horizontal arrows are theta-type links; vertical arrows are log-links. The lattice is not a commuting square of schemes. Horizontal and vertical arrows use incompatible pieces of local ring structure.
Introduction, local log-link paragraph At a nonarchimedean valuation, the log-link is modeled by applying log_v to local units: O×μ_Fv ⊗ Q -> F_v. The local group Pi_v is coric for the log-link, while the ring structure used to define the logarithm remains local to one vertical line.
Introduction, log-shell paragraph For bad nonarchimedean places, the log-shell is described as I_v = p_v^{-1} log_v(O×_Kv), a compact additive receptacle containing the Kummer images from both sides of the log-link. The log-shell gives an upper container rather than a precise equality. This is why later volume comparisons produce upper bounds.
Introduction, upper-semi discussion Iterates of the log-link and Kummer isomorphisms form diagrams that are only upper semi-compatible, not genuinely commutative. This is the source-level motivation for treating Ind3 separately in Theorem 3.11 and Corollary 3.12.
Introduction, coricity vocabulary Vertical coricity means invariance under log-links; horizontal coricity means invariance under theta-type links; bi-coricity means both. Later multiradial algorithms must name which direction of invariance they use. A single undifferentiated "invariant" flag would be misleading.
Introduction, Theorem 1.5 preview Compatibility of the log-link with the F_l^{±} symmetry produces vertically coric F⊢×μ prime-strips and bi-coric mono-analytic log-shells. These log-shells become the containers used in the multiradial LGP-monoid and tensor-packet construction of IUT III §3.

Horizontal direction

Horizontal arrows are theta-type links: Theta, Theta×μ, Gaussian Theta×μ_gau, and later LGP/lgp variants. They are not ring or scheme morphisms, and they are incompatible with the local ring structures that define logarithms.

Vertical direction

Vertical arrows are log-links. They apply local logarithmic structures inside a fixed arithmetic holomorphic structure, i.e. inside a single vertical line. This is the direction in which log-shells become available.

Coricity vocabulary

IUT III distinguishes vertical coricity, horizontal coricity, and bi-coricity. A structure is vertically coric if it is invariant along log-links, horizontally coric if invariant along theta-type links, and bi-coric if it survives both directions of the lattice.

Forced vertical step

To use log-shells after a horizontal theta-type link, the theory must move to a node that is a codomain of a vertical log-link. IUT III phrases this as every horizontal execution obligating a subsequent vertical execution.

Local log-link

The local log-link applies the p_v-adic logarithm to the unit quotient side of a local F-prime-strip. It uses the additive and multiplicative structures of the same local ring, so it is defined inside one arithmetic holomorphic structure.

This is why the vertical direction of the lattice is fundamentally different from a theta-type horizontal link.

IUT III Introductionlog-link

Log-shell containment

The bad-place log-shell I_v is designed to contain the images of the Kummer isomorphisms associated to both the domain and codomain of the log-link.

The containment is intentionally one-sided. It prepares an upper-bound mechanism for log-volumes rather than a statement that the two Kummer images are equal.

Remark 1.2.2upper container

Log-link/Kummer incompatibility

IUT III stresses that Kummer isomorphisms are not compatible with the coricity of the group-theoretically reconstructed data at the element level. The relevant compatibility is instead the upper-semi form obtained after passing to log-shells.

This prevents replacing Ind3 by an ordinary quotient-invariance assertion.

Proposition 1.2(iv)Ind3

Bi-coric log-shells

Theorem 1.5 is previewed in the introduction as producing vertically coric F⊢×μ-prime-strips and mono-analytic log-shells that are bi-coric under the lattice arrows.

These are the log-shell objects that later support splitting monoids of LGP-monoids and the tensor-packet comparison.

Theorem 1.5 previewbi-coricity
IUT III §1 source item Precise interface Audit consequence
Definition 1.1(i) For v in Vnon, the p-adic logarithm on the perfected unit monoid defines log(F_v), the tautological log-link F_v -> log(F_v), and the log-shell obtained by scaling the invariant pre-log-shell by (p_v^*)^{-1}. The nonarchimedean log-link is built from local units, group action, and the p-adic logarithm; it is not a map of the original full monoid.
Definition 1.1(ii) For v in Varc, a pointed universal cover of the groupification, quotients by roots of unity, and the complex logarithm construct log(F_v) and an archimedean log-shell. The archimedean case is Kummer/Aut-holomorphic and line-segment based; it should not be copied from the nonarchimedean formula.
Definition 1.1(iii) The local constructions assemble into a tautological log-link F -> log(F). A general or full log-link is obtained by post-composing with a poly-isomorphism log(F) -> F'. The full log-link deliberately replaces the tautological rigidifying path by poly-isomorphism data suitable for etale-like multiradial use.
Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi) The modules I_F⊢×μ ⊆ log(F⊢×μ) are constructed solely from the F⊢×μ-prime-strip, using only the unit quotient data. This is the source for treating log-shells as available after the F⊢×μ weakening used by theta-type links.
Remark 1.1.1 Iterates of log-links are understood only on the local unit groups in their domains. Non-unit/value-group parts are not needed to construct log-shells. The wiki keeps value-group distortion separate from log-shell construction: log-links render unit data additive.
Remark 1.1.2 The tautological log-link identifies the domain and codomain Pi_v's by a rigidifying path built from Frobenius-like monoids. The full log-link uses a tautologically indeterminate isomorphism. Multiradial algorithms need the full-log-link formulation because it can be expressed in etale-like terms without depending on the monoid path.
Proposition 1.2(i)-(iv) A log-link induces poly-isomorphisms of associated D- and D⊢-prime-strips, is compatible with ring structures and log-volumes, but the Kummer isomorphisms fail to be compatible with the D-coric comparison at element level. This is the exact reason that later log-Kummer compatibility must be upper-semi rather than equality-based.
Proposition 1.2(v)-(ix) Holomorphic, mono-analytic, and coric holomorphic log-shells are constructed with compatible log-volumes and poly-isomorphisms between D⊢, F⊢×μ, and F versions. The paper supplies several log-shell representations; they must not be collapsed into one undifferentiated shell object.
Proposition 1.2(x) Full log-links generate an oriented Frobenius-picture chain of F-prime-strips whose D and D⊢ cores are identified up to full poly-isomorphism. This vertical Frobenius-picture has translation symmetry fixing the core, but no arbitrary adjacent-vertex permutation symmetry.

Tautological and full log-links

Definition 1.1 first constructs a tautological log-link F -> log(F). A full log-link post-composes this with the full poly-isomorphism from log(F) to a target F-prime-strip.

Remark 1.1.2 explains the reason: the tautological link carries a specific monoid-built rigidifying path, while the full link is expressed in etale-like indeterminate terms needed later.

Definition 1.1(iii)Remark 1.1.2

Unit-only log-link domain

Remark 1.1.1 says that iterated log-links are to be understood on local units in their domains. The purpose of the log-link is to construct log-shells from units; non-unit value-group portions are not required for this construction.

This is the local mechanism behind the later split between additive log-shell data and multiplicative value-group distortion.

Remark 1.1.1units

F⊢×μ log-shells

Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi) reconstructs log-shell modules from F⊢×μ-prime-strip data alone. The construction uses the unit quotient/perfection interface and is unaffected by quotienting units by torsion.

This gives the log-shell side access to the weakened interface already used by the modified theta links of IUT II.

Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi)F⊢×μ

Mono-analytic log-shells

Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii) constructs mono-analytic log-shells from D⊢-prime-strip data and relates them by compatible poly-isomorphisms to F⊢×μ and holomorphic F-prime-strip log-shells.

These are the shell objects that can be read without carrying a specific arithmetic holomorphic history.

Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii)D⊢

Coric holomorphic log-shells

Proposition 1.2(ix) starts from a D-prime-strip, forms the corresponding F-prime-strip F(D), and uses the full log-link to construct coric holomorphic log-shells in the constant monoid data.

This is a separate representation from the mono-analytic shell, connected to it by the natural isomorphisms supplied in the proposition.

Proposition 1.2(ix)coric

Log-link Frobenius-picture

Proposition 1.2(x) organizes full log-links into an oriented integer-indexed chain. The associated D and D⊢ prime-strips form a fixed core under translation by Z.

As in the theta-link Frobenius-picture, arbitrary adjacent swaps are not allowed.

Proposition 1.2(x)Frobenius-picture
IUT III §1 source item Hodge-theater/lattice construction Reason it is proof-critical
Proposition 1.3(i) An isomorphism of associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters determines log-links on each constituent F-prime-strip of the theta and plus-minus bridges; the full case uses the full poly-isomorphism. The theater-level log-link is built over D-theater data. It is not chosen independently for each F-prime-strip without synchronization.
